Verified by unit test asserting on logger output. Daemon's logger held on daemonDeps.Logger (cmd/state_daemon.go:25); call site at line 149.

title: Split saver command constants into placeholder and daemon variants
description:
Problem: internal/tmux/portal_saver.go exposes a single portalSaverCommand = "portal state daemon" constant wiring both session-creation and pane-process responsibilities into the same string. Component F decouples those roles — session creation must run a benign placeholder so destroy-unattached=off can be applied before the daemon ever starts. We need named constants for both shapes before reordering in 3-2.

Solution: Replace portalSaverCommand with two named, in-source-documented constants:
- portalSaverPlaceholderCommand = "sh -c 'exec tail -f /dev/null'" (create time)
- portalSaverDaemonCommand = "portal state daemon" (respawn time)
Constants-only change — no behavioural change; existing call sites repointed at portalSaverDaemonCommand.

Outcome: Both constants exist with doc-comments capturing why placeholder is tail -f /dev/null rather than sleep infinity (BSD sleep rejects infinity on macOS) and what the daemon variant is for. portalSaverCommand is gone; go build ./... and go test ./internal/tmux/... pass with no behavioural diff.

Do:
- Edit internal/tmux/portal_saver.go: replace single portalSaverCommand (currently line 33) with two constants.
- Doc-comment placeholder: (1) exec tail -f /dev/null blocks indefinitely on macOS+Linux; (2) sleep infinity rejected (BSD sleep parse error); (3) placeholder structurally incapable of writing to state dir or contending for daemon lock; (4) lives until killed by respawn-pane -k or tmux kill-session.
- Doc-comment daemon: real pane process installed by respawn-pane -k after destroy-unattached=off in effect.
- Update existing call site in createPortalSaverWithRetry (line 399) to use portalSaverDaemonCommand — constants-only no-op diff.
- Leave PortalSaverName and other exported names untouched.

Acceptance Criteria:
- portalSaverPlaceholderCommand exists with literal value "sh -c 'exec tail -f /dev/null'" and doc-comment citing macOS BSD sleep rationale.
- portalSaverDaemonCommand exists with literal value "portal state daemon".
- No symbol named portalSaverCommand remains (grep returns nothing).
- createPortalSaverWithRetry references portalSaverDaemonCommand (no behavioural change from main).
- go build ./... succeeds; go test ./internal/tmux/... passes unchanged from main.
- PortalSaverName constant unchanged.

Tests:
- it exposes portalSaverPlaceholderCommand as the literal sh -c 'exec tail -f /dev/null'
- it exposes portalSaverDaemonCommand as the literal portal state daemon
- existing portal_saver unit tests pass unchanged

Edge Cases:
- macOS BSD sleep infinity rejection — rationale captured in-source.
- PortalSaverName export untouched — downstream packages import by name.
- Constants-only task: no behaviour change. Reordering happens in 3-2.

Context: Spec § Component F: sleep infinity rejected because BSD sleep on macOS exits immediately recreating the race. tail -f /dev/null blocks on both platforms.

Spec Reference: .workflows/slow-open-empty-previews-and-zombie-sessions/specification/slow-open-empty-previews-and-zombie-sessions/specification.md § Component F.

title: Reorder BootstrapPortalSaver to create-placeholder, set-option, respawn-daemon
description:
Problem: BootstrapPortalSaver (lines 266-288) creates _portal-saver with portal state daemon as initial pane process AND then sets destroy-unattached=off. If daemon exits between those two tmux calls (notably lock-loser case), tmux self-destroys the session before the option is applied, producing "no such session: _portal-saver" log noise + recovery doom-loop. Fix is to decouple session creation from daemon launch: create with placeholder, set option on stable session, respawn pane with real daemon command.

Solution: Rework create branch of BootstrapPortalSaver (and createPortalSaverWithRetry) to three-step ordering. Step (1) createPortalSaverWithRetry passes portalSaverPlaceholderCommand (from 3-1) to NewDetachedSessionNoCwd. Step (2) existing SetSessionOption(PortalSaverName, "destroy-unattached", "off") runs against placeholder session — guaranteed-alive because tail -f /dev/null does not exit. Step (3) c.RespawnPane(PortalSaverName, portalSaverDaemonCommand) replaces placeholder with real daemon. RespawnPane existing signature (RespawnPane(target, command string) error at internal/tmux/tmux.go:708) reused verbatim — no signature change. Readiness barrier (3-3) inserted as follow-on after respawn.

Outcome: After BootstrapPortalSaver returns on clean bootstrap, tmux list-panes -t _portal-saver -F '#{pane_pid}' + ps -o args= -p <pid> shows portal state daemon, and tmux show-options -t _portal-saver destroy-unattached reports off. Lock-loser exit does not destroy session. Zero "no such session: _portal-saver" log entries during bootstrap window.

Do:
- In internal/tmux/portal_saver.go: edit createPortalSaverWithRetry (line 396) — pass portalSaverPlaceholderCommand to NewDetachedSessionNoCwd. Retry/concurrency-race logic preserved.
- In BootstrapPortalSaver (line 266), restructure create flow to three-step order:
  1. (If creation needed) createPortalSaverWithRetry(c) — creates with placeholder.
  2. c.SetSessionOption(PortalSaverName, "destroy-unattached", "off") — runs against placeholder, BEFORE respawn. Preserve existing error-wrapping.
  3. New: c.RespawnPane(PortalSaverName, portalSaverDaemonCommand). On error wrap as "bootstrap _portal-saver: respawn daemon: %w" and return — respawn structurally required.
- Session-already-present-and-alive branch must NOT respawn — no-op happy path.
- Doc-comment function with new ordering and rationale.
- Readiness barrier (Task 3-3) layered after respawn — leave clearly-named extension point or stub call.
- Update portal_saver_test.go: fake Commander observes call sequence new-session ... 'sh -c exec tail -f /dev/null' → set-option ... destroy-unattached off → respawn-pane -k ... 'portal state daemon'.

Acceptance Criteria:
- createPortalSaverWithRetry calls NewDetachedSessionNoCwd(PortalSaverName, portalSaverPlaceholderCommand).
- In create branch, tmux call order: create-session → set-option destroy-unattached=off → respawn-pane.
- RespawnPane called with (PortalSaverName, portalSaverDaemonCommand); signature unchanged.
- SetSessionOption failure → wrapped error; RespawnPane failure → wrapped "respawn daemon" error.
- Session-present-and-alive happy path does NOT issue respawn (idempotency preserved).
- Concurrent-bootstrap race (HasSession true after failed new-session) still resolves as success.
- go vet ./... and go test ./internal/tmux/... pass.

Tests:
- BootstrapPortalSaver issues create-then-set-option-then-respawn in order on a clean bootstrap
- BootstrapPortalSaver does not respawn when session is present and daemon is alive
- BootstrapPortalSaver returns wrapped error when SetSessionOption fails
- BootstrapPortalSaver returns wrapped error when RespawnPane fails
- createPortalSaverWithRetry uses placeholder command
- concurrent-bootstrap race still treats existing session as success

Edge Cases:
- Lock-loser exit between respawn and next bootstrap: destroy-unattached=off already set, session persists.
- Concurrent bootstraps: HasSession re-probe in createPortalSaverWithRetry resolves race; respawn runs unconditionally post-creation.
- Session-present-and-alive skips respawn — daemon healthy.
- RespawnPane signature: confirm shape at impl start; spec authorises adapting only if differs.

Context: Spec § Component F: 1. Create saver with benign placeholder. 2. Set destroy-unattached=off. 3. respawn-pane -k -t _portal-saver 'portal state daemon'. Placeholder structurally incapable of running portal logic. Window between create and respawn bounded by two tmux command latencies (<50ms).

Spec Reference: .workflows/slow-open-empty-previews-and-zombie-sessions/specification/slow-open-empty-previews-and-zombie-sessions/specification.md § Component F.

title: Add post-respawn readiness barrier polling daemon.pid + state.IdentifyDaemon
description:
Problem: After respawn-pane -k installs the daemon in step (3) of new ordering (Task 3-2), daemon needs finite warm-up before daemon.pid written + lock acquired. Subsequent bootstrap steps (Restore, EagerSignalHydrate) assume healthy daemon. Without readiness barrier they race the respawn — observing transient absent-pid or not-yet-identified states that mask real daemon failures.

Solution: After respawn call in BootstrapPortalSaver, poll for daemon.pid to exist AND state.IdentifyDaemon to return IdentifyIsPortalDaemon. Bounded to 2s total at 50ms cadence. On success return nil. On timeout log WARN under ComponentBootstrap with literal "saver respawn: daemon did not come up within 2s" and return nil (best-effort). On transient IdentifyDaemon errors retry; IdentifyDead retries (daemon will rewrite PID file post-fork); IdentifyNotPortalDaemon only resolved via deadline (recycled PID rare case).

Outcome: BootstrapPortalSaver's create branch returns only after daemon has written daemon.pid AND state.IdentifyDaemon confirms it as live portal state daemon, OR after 2s elapsed (single WARN, returns nil). Subsequent orchestrator steps observe stable daemon in happy path.

Do:
- Add helper to internal/tmux/portal_saver.go: waitForSaverDaemonReady(stateDir string) error.
- Add package-level seams (mirroring kill-barrier pattern lines 113-134):
  - saverReadinessReadPID = state.ReadPIDFile
  - saverReadinessIdentify = state.IdentifyDaemon
  - saverReadinessPollInterval = 50 * time.Millisecond (var)
  - saverReadinessTimeout = 2 * time.Second (var)
- Reuse killBarrierLogger (existing BarrierLogger) as WARN sink — same observable shape.
- Implementation:
  1. deadline := time.Now().Add(saverReadinessTimeout).
  2. Loop with time.NewTicker(saverReadinessPollInterval):
     - pid, err := saverReadinessReadPID(stateDir). On ErrPIDFileAbsent / any read error: continue.
     - result, err := saverReadinessIdentify(pid). If err != nil (transient): continue. If result == IdentifyIsPortalDaemon: return nil. Otherwise (Dead, NotPortalDaemon): continue.
     - If !time.Now().Before(deadline): emit WARN via killBarrierLogger.Warn(state.ComponentBootstrap, "saver respawn: daemon did not come up within %v", saverReadinessTimeout), return nil.
- Call waitForSaverDaemonReady(stateDir) from BootstrapPortalSaver immediately after successful RespawnPane (only create branch; not session-present-and-alive branch).
- Doc-comment helper with success/timeout contract.

Acceptance Criteria:
- waitForSaverDaemonReady(stateDir string) error exists in internal/tmux/portal_saver.go.
- Polls at 50ms cadence; total bounded to 2s via deadline computed once at entry.
- Returns nil immediately when ReadPIDFile returns PID AND IdentifyDaemon returns IdentifyIsPortalDaemon.
- On timeout, emits exactly one WARN via killBarrierLogger.Warn under ComponentBootstrap containing "saver respawn: daemon did not come up within"; returns nil.
- Treats IdentifyDead, IdentifyNotPortalDaemon, ErrPIDFileAbsent, transient ps errors, PID-file read errors as not-ready — continues polling.
- Called from BootstrapPortalSaver immediately after RespawnPane (create branch only).
- Package-level seams individually overridable from tests.

Tests:
- waitForSaverDaemonReady returns nil immediately when PID present and identifies as portal daemon
- waitForSaverDaemonReady retries while daemon.pid is absent then succeeds
- waitForSaverDaemonReady retries on transient IdentifyDaemon ps failure
- waitForSaverDaemonReady retries on IdentifyDead until next pid write
- waitForSaverDaemonReady times out and emits WARN when daemon never identifies
- waitForSaverDaemonReady total wall-clock is bounded by saverReadinessTimeout
- BootstrapPortalSaver invokes waitForSaverDaemonReady after RespawnPane on create path

Edge Cases:
- daemon.pid missing in early ticks — not-ready, loop continues.
- IdentifyDaemon transient ps failure — retried until deadline.
- IdentifyNotPortalDaemon (recycled PID) — only deadline resolves; spec requires.
- IdentifyDead — retried; daemon may rewrite PID file post-fork.
- Best-effort return on timeout: WARN logged, nil returned, orchestrator proceeds.
- 2s ceiling enforced by deadline computed once, not tick counting — clock-skew safe.

Context: Spec § Component F readiness barrier: poll for daemon.pid AND state.IdentifyDaemon to return IdentifyIsPortalDaemon. Bounded 2s total, 50ms cadence. On timeout: WARN "saver respawn: daemon did not come up within 2s", return — best-effort. Phase 1 shipped state.IdentifyDaemon.

Spec Reference: .workflows/slow-open-empty-previews-and-zombie-sessions/specification/slow-open-empty-previews-and-zombie-sessions/specification.md § Component F (Readiness barrier).

title: Compose unhealthy-saver recreate path with new ordering
description:
Problem: When BootstrapPortalSaver encounters an existing saver session with dead daemon (lines 269-275), it calls killSaverAndWaitForDaemonFn and falls through to recreate. After 3-2 the recreate path is create-placeholder, set-option, respawn-daemon — we need the unhealthy-saver path to compose cleanly with that new ordering, including case where a prior bootstrap crashed mid-respawn and saver currently hosts the placeholder process (not a real daemon). BootstrapAliveCheck correctly reports this as unhealthy (no live daemon.pid), so existing kill-and-recreate path runs — need to verify composition + EnsurePortalSaverVersion callers still delegate correctly.

Solution: Audit unhealthy-saver branch of BootstrapPortalSaver and ensure it falls through to new create-placeholder → set-option → respawn-daemon → readiness-barrier sequence built in 3-2/3-3. No new code path required if 3-2 already routes recreate fall-through correctly. Add explicit unit coverage for placeholder-leak scenario (prior bootstrap crashed between F's steps 2-3) and verify EnsurePortalSaverVersion still delegates through BootstrapPortalSaver unchanged.

Outcome: Saver session lingering with just placeholder (no live daemon) is detected by BootstrapAliveCheck, killed via killSaverAndWaitForDaemonFn, recreated via new ordering. No persistent placeholder leak survives any single recovery bootstrap. EnsurePortalSaverVersion remains thin wrapper around BootstrapPortalSaver — its kill+delegate flow inherits new ordering by composition.

Do:
- Read BootstrapPortalSaver after 3-2/3-3 changes have landed. Confirm unhealthy-saver branch (lines 269-275 current main) falls through to new ordering. If fall-through routes through createPortalSaverWithRetry + set-option + respawn + readiness, no code change — task is verifying composition + adding coverage.
- If code change IS needed, apply minimal patch and document inline.
- Confirm EnsurePortalSaverVersion (line 328) still delegates to BootstrapPortalSaver and inherits new ordering. No signature changes; matrix-driven kill decision unchanged.
- Update/add internal/tmux/portal_saver_test.go unit cases for:
  - Saver exists with placeholder process only — BootstrapAliveCheck stubbed false; assert kill → create-placeholder → set-option → respawn → readiness sequence.
  - kill-session on unhealthy saver targets placeholder — argv-recording fake observes kill-session before new-session.
  - EnsurePortalSaverVersion routes unhealthy-saver kill through new ordering — version mismatch + alive=false (no kill row) and alive=true+mismatch (kill row); both reach BootstrapPortalSaver and observe new ordering.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Unhealthy-saver branch falls through cleanly to 3-2 ordering — verified by call-order assertion in unit test.
- Placeholder-only-lingering saver recycled via killSaverAndWaitForDaemonFn → recreate path; no infinite-loop or panic.
- EnsurePortalSaverVersion delegates to BootstrapPortalSaver with unchanged signature and inherits new ordering.
- kill-session argv recorded before subsequent new-session 'sh -c exec tail -f /dev/null' argv.
- No persistent placeholder leak — single recovery bootstrap restores daemon.
- go test ./internal/tmux/... passes including new placeholder-leak coverage.

Tests:
- BootstrapPortalSaver recycles a placeholder-only saver via kill+recreate with new ordering
- EnsurePortalSaverVersion alive=true+mismatch flows through new BootstrapPortalSaver ordering
- EnsurePortalSaverVersion alive=false+anything skips kill and still uses new ordering
- No persistent placeholder leak across single recovery cycle

Edge Cases:
- Placeholder-only saver from crashed prior bootstrap presents as unhealthy via BootstrapAliveCheck (daemon.pid absent/stale) — existing alive-check handles it; no new logic.
- kill-session targets placeholder (only pane process at that moment) — KillSession doesn't care.
- EnsurePortalSaverVersion not modified — kill decision matrix correct; delegation to BootstrapPortalSaver carries new ordering for free.
- Tolerance of KillSession errors in unhealthy-saver branch preserved (leading _ = discard intentional).

Context: Spec § Component F interaction with kill-barrier: When BootstrapPortalSaver encounters existing saver with dead daemon (BootstrapAliveCheck returns false), it calls killSaverAndWaitForDaemonFn and falls through to recreate. With A+B in place, kill phase reliable; recreate path now uses placeholder-then-respawn. No persistent placeholder leak: even if bootstrap crashes between F steps 2-3, next bootstrap sees unhealthy saver and recovers via existing path.

Spec Reference: .workflows/slow-open-empty-previews-and-zombie-sessions/specification/slow-open-empty-previews-and-zombie-sessions/specification.md § Component F (Interaction with kill-barrier, No persistent placeholder leak).

title: Integration test for clean bootstrap end-state and lock-loser persistence
description:
Problem: Unit tests prove call sequence is correct but do not prove actual end-state against real tmux server. Spec acceptance criteria require: (a) zero "no such session: _portal-saver" log entries during clean bootstrap; (b) tmux show-options reports destroy-unattached=off AND pane process is portal state daemon; (c) lock-loser exit does NOT destroy the session. Integration-level invariants.

Solution: Real-tmux integration test in internal/tmux/portal_saver_integration_test.go using tmuxtest.New + portalbintest.BuildPortalBinary + portaltest.NewIsolatedStateEnv (Phase 1). Invokes BootstrapPortalSaver against fresh state dir, asserts end-state observables, then simulates lock-loser by seeding a competing daemon and asserts session persists after lock-loser daemon exits.

Outcome: Two real-tmux integration test functions land:
- TestBootstrapPortalSaver_CleanBootstrap_EndState — proves clean-bootstrap end-state.
- TestBootstrapPortalSaver_LockLoser_SessionPersists — proves lock-loser exit does not destroy session.
Both pass with tmux installed and fail when saver-creation ordering regresses.

Do:
- TestBootstrapPortalSaver_CleanBootstrap_EndState:
  1. tmuxtest.SkipIfNoTmux(t); build portal binary via portalbintest.BuildPortalBinary(t); stage on PATH via portalbintest.StagePortalBinary(t, bin).
  2. env, stateDir := portaltest.NewIsolatedStateEnv(t) — Phase 1 helper.
  3. sock := tmuxtest.New(t); construct *tmux.Client; spawned tmux server inherits isolated env.
  4. Pre-condition: assert _portal-saver not present.
  5. Call tmux.BootstrapPortalSaver(client, stateDir); assert no error.
  6. Post-condition (poll up to 2s for daemon readiness):
     - client.HasSession(PortalSaverName) returns true.
     - tmux show-options -t _portal-saver destroy-unattached contains "off".
     - tmux list-panes -t _portal-saver -F '#{pane_pid}' returns pid; ps -o args= -p <pid> contains "portal state daemon" (not "tail -f /dev/null").
     - Log scan: capture WARN/ERROR logger output and assert zero "no such session: _portal-saver" entries.
  7. Cleanup via t.Cleanup.
- TestBootstrapPortalSaver_LockLoser_SessionPersists:
  1. Same scaffolding.
  2. Pre-seed competing daemon: spawn portal state daemon against same isolated stateDir (exec.Command with cmd.Env = env); wait until it writes daemon.pid AND state.IdentifyDaemon confirms (loop ≤2s).
  3. Invoke tmux.BootstrapPortalSaver(client, stateDir). New daemon will lose lock (Component C) and exit cleanly.
  4. Wait up to 2s for respawned daemon to exit.
  5. Assert client.HasSession(PortalSaverName) still returns true — destroy-unattached=off prevents auto-destroy.
  6. Cleanup: kill seeded competing daemon (or rely on tmuxtest's kill-server SIGHUPping everything).
- Place under existing integration test conventions; no t.Parallel.

Acceptance Criteria:
- TestBootstrapPortalSaver_CleanBootstrap_EndState exists in internal/tmux/portal_saver_integration_test.go (or sibling); skips when tmux absent.
- Uses portaltest.NewIsolatedStateEnv(t) — Phase 1 backstop guarantees no developer state mutation.
- Asserts: HasSession true; show-options destroy-unattached contains "off"; pane pid → ps args containing "portal state daemon"; zero "no such session: _portal-saver" log entries.
- TestBootstrapPortalSaver_LockLoser_SessionPersists exists and proves lock-loser exit does not destroy saver session.
- Both tests fail if 3-2 ordering reverted (verify by temporarily reordering during implementation).
- No t.Parallel.

Tests:
- TestBootstrapPortalSaver_CleanBootstrap_EndState verifies destroy-unattached=off and daemon pane process on clean bootstrap
- TestBootstrapPortalSaver_CleanBootstrap_EndState emits zero 'no such session: _portal-saver' log entries
- TestBootstrapPortalSaver_LockLoser_SessionPersists keeps the saver alive after a lock-loser daemon exits
- both integration tests skip cleanly when tmux is absent

Edge Cases:
- Test without tmux on PATH — skipped via tmuxtest.SkipIfNoTmux.
- Test without portal binary — built fresh via portalbintest.BuildPortalBinary, PATH-staged.
- Developer real state dir untouched — guaranteed by portaltest.NewIsolatedStateEnv + fingerprint-diff t.Cleanup.
- Lock-loser timing: seeded competing daemon must be confirmed alive AND owning lock before bootstrap fires.
- Respawned daemon's pane pid observed via list-panes -F after readiness barrier (in lock-loser test, barrier may time out — log-scan accommodates timeout WARN, distinct from "no such session").
- destroy-unattached=off must be set BEFORE lock-loser exits — that's the invariant being proved.

Context: Spec § Component F acceptance: No "no such session" log line on create. destroy-unattached=off set before daemon can exit. Lock-loser daemon does not destroy session: simulate lock-loser (another daemon holds singleton); new bootstrap creates _portal-saver, applies destroy-unattached=off, respawns daemon, daemon exits as lock-loser, _portal-saver remains. Verified by integration test.

Spec Reference: .workflows/slow-open-empty-previews-and-zombie-sessions/specification/slow-open-empty-previews-and-zombie-sessions/specification.md § Component F Acceptance criteria bullets 1-3.

title: Integration test for environment inheritance parity across respawn
description:
Problem: Component F changes saver's initial pane process from portal state daemon to placeholder + respawn. Spec § Component F Environment inheritance across respawn requires respawned daemon sees same environment it would have seen as the original initial pane command pre-F. If respawn-pane did NOT inherit session's env (or NewDetachedSessionNoCwd grew -e KEY=VAL overrides), daemon could behave differently — e.g., reading wrong XDG_CONFIG_HOME and corrupting different state directory.

Solution: Real-tmux integration test that verifies tmux show-environment -t _portal-saver output for XDG_CONFIG_HOME, HOME, PATH is identical between (a) pre-F-style baseline (session with daemon command directly, captured before respawn) and (b) post-F path (placeholder + respawn). The two environments must match exactly for those keys.

Outcome: New integration test TestBootstrapPortalSaver_EnvironmentInheritanceAcrossRespawn lives in internal/tmux/portal_saver_integration_test.go. Computes pre-F baseline and post-F observed env, asserts byte-for-byte equality on XDG_CONFIG_HOME, HOME, PATH lines. Fails if NewDetachedSessionNoCwd grows env overrides that diverge across respawn.

Do:
- Add TestBootstrapPortalSaver_EnvironmentInheritanceAcrossRespawn:
  1. tmuxtest.SkipIfNoTmux(t); build + PATH-stage portal via portalbintest.
  2. env, _ := portaltest.NewIsolatedStateEnv(t).
  3. sock := tmuxtest.New(t); tmux server inherits isolated env.
  4. Pre-F baseline: create throwaway session via client.NewDetachedSessionNoCwd("_env-baseline", "sh -c 'exec tail -f /dev/null'"). Read tmux show-environment -t _env-baseline via Commander.RunRaw, parse lines for three keys. Kill _env-baseline immediately after capture.
  5. Post-F observed: call tmux.BootstrapPortalSaver(client, stateDir) which executes full create-placeholder → set-option → respawn flow. After readiness barrier returns, read tmux show-environment -t _portal-saver. Parse same three keys.
  6. Assertion: three key/value pairs in baseline equal three in observed. Pretty-print both maps on diff.
  7. Independently assert NewDetachedSessionNoCwd called WITHOUT -e KEY=VAL overrides: inspect argv via recorder or unit test in portal_saver_test.go.
- Use existing tmux.Commander.RunRaw for show-environment (verbatim output).
- Place under existing integration test conventions; no t.Parallel.

Acceptance Criteria:
- TestBootstrapPortalSaver_EnvironmentInheritanceAcrossRespawn exists; skips when tmux absent.
- Captures XDG_CONFIG_HOME, HOME, PATH from pre-F baseline AND from _portal-saver post-F; asserts byte-equality.
- Fails with clear diff message if any key differs.
- Complementary unit test (or in-test argv inspection) asserts NewDetachedSessionNoCwd constructs new-session -d -s NAME [SHELLCMD] with NO -e KEY=VAL overrides.
- Uses portaltest.NewIsolatedStateEnv.
- go test ./internal/tmux/... passes with tmux available.

Tests:
- TestBootstrapPortalSaver_EnvironmentInheritanceAcrossRespawn asserts XDG_CONFIG_HOME / HOME / PATH parity between pre-F baseline and post-F observed
- NewDetachedSessionNoCwd argv contains no -e KEY=VAL env override

Edge Cases:
- tmux show-environment for unset vars reports leading -VARNAME (no =) — treat unset symmetrically (both must agree on unset-ness).
- PATH may include test-staged binary directories — fine if both inherit same tmux server's env.
- Baseline session must be killed BEFORE saver bootstrap fires.
- Equality compare on per-key map, not raw byte-equality — only assert three daemon-relevant keys.
- NewDetachedSessionNoCwd source today (line 372-380) constructs [new-session -d -s name shellCommand] with no -e — assertion future-proofs.

Context: Spec § Component F environment inheritance: respawn-pane runs new process with session's environment (preserved from new-session time + set-environment updates). createPortalSaverWithRetry calls NewDetachedSessionNoCwd without -e KEY=VAL overrides — saver inherits tmux server's env as-is. F preserves this. Acceptance: tmux show-environment -t _portal-saver produces output identical to pre-F baseline for any env var daemon reads (XDG_CONFIG_HOME, HOME, PATH).

Spec Reference: .workflows/slow-open-empty-previews-and-zombie-sessions/specification/slow-open-empty-previews-and-zombie-sessions/specification.md § Component F (Environment inheritance across respawn).

title: Add SIGKILL escalation to killSaverAndWaitForDaemon with identity-check
description:
Component A — Kill-barrier escalation. See full detail in .workflows/slow-open-empty-previews-and-zombie-sessions/planning/slow-open-empty-previews-and-zombie-sessions/phase-4-tasks.md task 4-1.

Problem: When recorded daemon.pid references a portal state daemon NOT the _portal-saver pane process (orphan with different parent tmux server), tmux kill-session _portal-saver cannot reach it. Current killSaverAndWaitForDaemon polls for full 5s window, times out, adds guaranteed 5s ceiling to every portal open invocation.

Solution: After existing session-kill poll loop times out, identity-check priorPID via state.IdentifyDaemon immediately before sending SIGKILL directly. Skip SIGTERM (orphan's view divergent — must not give shutdown handler chance to fire destructive captureAndCommit + gcOrphanScrollback). Post-SIGKILL poll 50ms cadence, 1s budget. Persistent aliveness → WARN, proceed (best-effort).

Do:
- Edit internal/tmux/portal_saver.go — extend killSaverAndWaitForDaemon (lines 212-248).
- Replace WARN+return at lines 238-244 with escalation path:
  1. state.IdentifyDaemon(priorPID). Non-IdentifyIsPortalDaemon (Dead, NotPortalDaemon, err≠nil) → emit WARN "prior daemon (pid=%d) not identity-checked as portal state daemon; skipping SIGKILL" via killBarrierLogger, return nil.
  2. IdentifyIsPortalDaemon → IMMEDIATELY syscall.Kill(priorPID, SIGKILL). No statement between identity check and kill.
  3. Bounded poll: 50ms ticker (killBarrierPollInterval), new killBarrierEscalationTimeout=1s. On !killBarrierIsAlive(priorPID) return nil. On deadline: WARN "prior daemon (pid=%d) survived SIGKILL escalation within %v", return nil.
- Add seams: killBarrierSendSIGKILL = func(pid int) error { return syscall.Kill(pid, syscall.SIGKILL) }, killBarrierIdentifyDaemon = state.IdentifyDaemon. Reset via t.Cleanup.
- Production legitimate-daemon path unchanged: SIGHUP from tmux kill-session still drives defaultShutdownFlush for saver-pane daemon (PID reachable via session-kill, exits inside 5s poll).

Acceptance Criteria:
- Returns within ~6s when priorPID is orphan (5s session-kill + 1s SIGKILL).
- SIGKILL sent only when IdentifyIsPortalDaemon; all other outcomes skip signal and return nil.
- Identity-check is immediate previous statement to SIGKILL syscall.
- Post-SIGKILL poll: 50ms cadence, 1s total budget.
- Persistent aliveness → exactly one WARN, returns nil.
- Legitimate saver-pane SIGHUP path bytes-identical pre-task.
- No SIGTERM ever sent.

Tests (10 cases): identity-checks-as-portal-daemon-then-SIGKILLs; IdentifyDead-no-kill; IdentifyNotPortalDaemon-no-kill; transient-error-no-kill; SIGKILL-succeeds-poll-observes-death; SIGKILL-process-survives-WARN; identity-check-is-immediate-predecessor; never-sends-SIGTERM; priorPID-dies-during-session-kill-poll; no-daemon.pid-file-tolerant-kill.

Edge Cases:
- PID recycled between poll and identity-check — refusal covers it.
- PID recycled between identity-check and SIGKILL — accepted residual µs window.
- Transient ps err → skip SIGKILL.
- SIGKILL ESRCH (already dead) → success.
- Legitimate daemon SIGHUP path: pane dies inside 5s poll, escalation never runs.

Spec Reference: § Component A — Kill-Barrier Escalation.

title: Add no-final-flush snapshot test for escalation-killed orphans
description:
Component A correctness hinges on orphan NOT running final captureAndCommit/gcOrphanScrollback on its way out — that final flush against divergent view is the destructive operation. Spec mandates empirical assertion that scrollback dir is bytes-identical immediately before SIGKILL and 200ms after orphan exits. See phase-4-tasks.md task 4-2 for full detail.

Solution: Integration test spawns real orphan portal state daemon against isolated state dir, snapshots scrollback dir immediately before SIGKILL, polls for process death, snapshots again 200ms after death, asserts snapshots byte-identical (no new .bin, no removed .bin, no size/mtime/content delta).

Do:
- Place test in internal/tmux/portal_saver_escalation_integration_test.go (or cmd/bootstrap/) under integration build tag.
- Use portaltest.NewIsolatedStateEnv(t) (Phase 1) for isolation + fingerprint-diff t.Cleanup backstop.
- Use portalbintest.BuildPortalBinary.
- Spawn portal state daemon subprocess with isolated env against real tmux fixture (tmuxtest) with _portal-saver whose pane is OTHER process (orphan is NOT the pane).
- Wait until orphan produces ≥1 .bin in <stateDir>/scrollback/ (poll os.ReadDir, bounded 3s).
- Snapshot: map[string]fileFingerprint keyed by relative path (existence, size, mtime ns, ctime ns, SHA-256). Reuse Phase 1 fingerprint type — do not redefine locally.
- Send SIGKILL directly (mimicking escalation; do not go through helper).
- Poll os.FindProcess + syscall.Kill(pid, 0) until gone, sleep exactly 200ms.
- Snapshot again. Assert deep equality (or per-field comparison with precise diff on failure).
- Test must FAIL against pre-Task-4-1 code with escalation accidentally routed through SIGTERM (would invoke defaultShutdownFlush and produce delta).

Acceptance Criteria:
- Compiles under integration build tag.
- Uses portaltest.NewIsolatedStateEnv.
- Pre-SIGKILL snapshot non-empty (≥1 .bin); test does not silently pass against empty.
- Post-SIGKILL snapshot taken exactly 200ms after death observed.
- Equality asserted across all 5 fingerprint fields; any delta fails with path-and-field diagnostic.
- Passes against 4-1 SIGKILL escalation, FAILS against hypothetical SIGTERM variant.

Tests:
- escalation-killed orphan produces no scrollback delta in 200ms post-death window
- snapshot diff fails on deliberately-modified .bin (meta-test guarding diff implementation)

Edge Cases:
- Orphan exits before snapshot taken — fails with clear "snapshot never taken" rather than silent green.
- _portal-saver disappears between fixture setup and snapshot — tmux fixture pins session.
- Filesystem clock granularity coarser than 1ms — mtime at ns granularity (ModTime().UnixNano()); no-write window shows identical ns.
- Symlinks in scrollback — fingerprint uses lstat (Phase 1 helper spec).

Spec Reference: § Component A acceptance criterion — no final-flush GC cycle on escalation-killed orphans; snapshot before SIGKILL + 200ms after exit must be identical.

title: Implement SweepOrphanDaemons core (pgrep + legitimate-set + identity + kill)
description:
Component B core. Component A only handles single PID recorded in daemon.pid. When multiple orphans exist concurrently, A cannot reach orphans beyond the recorded one. No bootstrap-time enumeration today. See phase-4-tasks.md task 4-3 for full detail.

Solution: Standalone SweepOrphanDaemons function: (1) enumerates candidate PIDs via pgrep -fx '^portal state daemon( |$)'; (2) builds legitimate set from _portal-saver pane PID (empty when absent); (3) for each non-legitimate PID identity-checks via state.IdentifyDaemon, SIGKILLs survivors; (4) logs INFO per killed PID, WARN-and-swallow on every error. Best-effort by construction — never aborts bootstrap.

Do:
- Create cmd/bootstrap/orphan_sweep.go defining OrphanSweeper interface + *OrphanSweepCore (mirror MarkerCleaner/FIFOSweeper pattern in cmd/bootstrap/stale_marker_cleanup.go).
- Interface: type OrphanSweeper interface { SweepOrphanDaemons() error }.
- *OrphanSweepCore seams:
  - Pgrep func() ([]int, error) — pgrep -fx '^portal state daemon( |$)'. Production wiring in adapter (4-4).
  - SaverPanePID func() (int, error) — pane PID of _portal-saver; on absent or call failing returns (0, nil).
  - Identify func(pid int) (state.IdentifyResult, error) — defaults to state.IdentifyDaemon.
  - Kill func(pid int) error — defaults to syscall.Kill(pid, SIGKILL).
  - Logger bootstrap.Logger.
- Algorithm:
  1. Pgrep(). On error: WARN, return nil.
  2. SaverPanePID(). On error: WARN ("sweep: list-panes _portal-saver failed, legitimate set empty: %v"), treat as empty. Build legitimate set.
  3. For each candidate PID NOT in legitimate set AND ≠ os.Getpid():
     - Identify(pid). On err: WARN, continue. On non-IdentifyIsPortalDaemon: DEBUG, continue.
     - Kill(pid). On err: WARN, continue. On success: INFO "sweep: killed orphan daemon pid=%d".
  4. Return nil unconditionally.
- Canonical pgrep form. Do NOT use ps-based form.
- Add unit tests in cmd/bootstrap/orphan_sweep_test.go driving every branch via seams (no real subprocesses, no real tmux).

Acceptance Criteria:
- OrphanSweeper interface + *OrphanSweepCore in cmd/bootstrap/orphan_sweep.go.
- All four seams injectable.
- On Pgrep error: WARN, returns nil.
- On SaverPanePID error or _portal-saver absent: legitimate empty, sweep proceeds against ALL pgrep results.
- For each non-legitimate PID with IdentifyIsPortalDaemon: SIGKILL sent, INFO emitted.
- For each non-legitimate PID with any other identity result: NO signal sent.
- All errors swallowed; method never returns non-nil error.
- os.Getpid() never SIGKILLed.

Tests (11): kills 2 orphans leaves legitimate; _portal-saver absent → kill all that identify; pgrep err logs WARN; list-panes err treats legitimate empty; IdentifyDead skipped; IdentifyNotPortalDaemon skipped; Identify transient skipped; Kill err logs WARN continues; clean state zero INFO; never-SIGTERM; os.Getpid() defensive skip.

Edge Cases:
- pgrep empty list → no-op.
- pgrep only legitimate → no-op, zero INFO.
- Legitimate empty + pgrep returns identifying PID → that PID IS killed.
- New daemon spawns between Pgrep and Kill → out of scope (next bootstrap will sweep).
- pgrep -fx status 1 with zero matches → treat as empty list, NOT error.

Spec Reference: § Component B — Bootstrap-Time Orphan Sweep.

title: Wire SweepOrphanDaemons as orchestrator step 4 between Set @portal-restoring and EnsureSaver
description:
Component B wiring. *OrphanSweepCore from 4-3 is dead code until wired into orchestrator. Spec requires running as new step between current step 3 (Set @portal-restoring) and current step 4 (EnsureSaver). Orchestrator becomes 11 steps. See phase-4-tasks.md task 4-4 for full detail.

Solution: Add OrphanSweeper field to Orchestrator struct in cmd/bootstrap/bootstrap.go. Invoke in Run between step 3 and step 4. Renumber 4-10 → 5-11 in package docstring + inline comments. Wire production seams in internal/bootstrapadapter/adapters.go. Update CLAUDE.md.

Do:
- Edit cmd/bootstrap/bootstrap.go:
  - Add OrphanSweeper OrphanSweeper field to Orchestrator.
  - Update package docstring with new 11-step ordering.
  - Insert in Run after step 3 (Restoring.Set()) and before step 4 (Saver.EnsureSaver()):
    // Step 4 — SweepOrphanDaemons (best-effort).
    o.Logger.Debug(state.ComponentBootstrap, "step 4 (SweepOrphanDaemons): entering")
    if err := o.OrphanSweeper.SweepOrphanDaemons(); err != nil {
        o.Logger.Warn(state.ComponentBootstrap, "step 4 (SweepOrphanDaemons) failed: %v", err)
    }
  - Renumber: EnsureSaver→5, Restore→6, EagerSignalHydrate→7, Clear @portal-restoring→8, CleanStaleMarkers→9, SweepOrphanFIFOs→10, CleanStale→11. Update debug log strings + docstring.
- Edit internal/bootstrapadapter/adapters.go (or new orphan_sweep.go):
  - Add NewOrphanSweeper(client *tmux.Client, logger *state.Logger) bootstrap.OrphanSweeper.
  - Production Pgrep: exec.Command("pgrep", "-fx", "^portal state daemon( |$)"). Status 1 + empty stdout = "no matches" NOT error. Other non-zero = error.
  - Production SaverPanePID: invoke client.ListPanesInSession(tmux.PortalSaverName), return first pane's PID, or new helper. On absent: (0, nil).
  - Production Identify: default state.IdentifyDaemon.
  - Production Kill: default syscall.Kill(pid, SIGKILL).
- Edit cmd/root.go (or cmd/bootstrap_production.go) to populate OrphanSweeper.
- Update CLAUDE.md "Server bootstrap" section: "ten-step" → "eleven-step"; insert new step 4; shift subsequent entries.
- No bootstrap.Warning added — best-effort per spec; only Logger.Warn.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Orchestrator.OrphanSweeper field exists; invoked once in Run between Restoring.Set() and Saver.EnsureSaver().
- Package docstring documents 11 steps with new step 4.
- Debug log step labels match new numbering.
- Production adapter NewOrphanSweeper exists in internal/bootstrapadapter; wires real pgrep + tmux list-panes + state.IdentifyDaemon + syscall.Kill.
- Production wiring in orchestrator construction populates OrphanSweeper.
- CLAUDE.md reflects 11-step ordering.
- On step error: WARN logged, continues — never aborts.
- Existing orchestrator unit tests updated with no-op OrphanSweeper.

Tests:
- step 4 invokes OrphanSweeper.SweepOrphanDaemons exactly once
- step 4 runs after Restoring.Set and before Saver.EnsureSaver (call-order recorded by spy)
- step 4 returning non-nil error logs WARN and does not abort
- step 4 returning nil emits no WARN entries
- nil OrphanSweeper field behaviour matches existing nil-interface convention
- existing step ordering 1-3 and 5-11 preserved after insertion

Edge Cases:
- OrphanSweeper field nil at construction — match existing convention.
- Adapter pgrep status 1 + empty stdout → empty slice, no error.
- SaverPanePID when HasSession(_portal-saver) false → (0, nil).
- Re-numbering risk: inline log strings hard-code "step 4" — acceptable.

Spec Reference: § Component B — invariants: SweepOrphanDaemons runs BEFORE EnsureSaver. 11-step list: 1.EnsureServer 2.RegisterPortalHooks 3.Set@portal-restoring 4.SweepOrphanDaemons(new) 5.EnsureSaver 6.Restore 7.EagerSignalHydrate 8.Clear 9.CleanStaleMarkers 10.SweepOrphanFIFOs 11.CleanStale.

title: Integration test for SweepOrphanDaemons (3 daemons converge to 1, clean state sends zero signals)
description:
Component B end-to-end coverage. Task 4-3 unit tests prove logic via seams; spec requires integration test against real pgrep + real state.IdentifyDaemon + real SIGKILL pipeline against real subprocesses + isolated state dir. See phase-4-tasks.md task 4-5 for full detail.

Solution: Integration test spawns 3 real portal state daemon subprocesses against isolated state dir + real tmux fixture, invokes production SweepOrphanDaemons, asserts pgrep -fxc 'portal state daemon' converges to 1. Second scenario: clean state (only legitimate saver-pane daemon) emits zero "sweep: killed orphan daemon" log entries.

Do:
- Place in cmd/bootstrap/orphan_sweep_integration_test.go under integration build tag.
- Use portaltest.NewIsolatedStateEnv(t).
- Use portalbintest.BuildPortalBinary.
- Use tmuxtest for real tmux server with _portal-saver + one user session.
- Scenario A — 3 daemons converge to 1:
  - Start legitimate saver-pane daemon via _portal-saver with portal state daemon as pane (or via BootstrapPortalSaver post-Phase-3).
  - Spawn 2 orphan daemons as direct subprocesses (not via tmux), isolated env, same stateDir. Parent process is test, NOT saver pane — they ARE orphans.
  - Confirm pgrep -fxc returns 3 in pre-state.
  - Construct production OrphanSweepCore via internal/bootstrapadapter.NewOrphanSweeper.
  - Invoke SweepOrphanDaemons().
  - Poll pgrep -fxc until 1, bounded 3s. Fail with current count if no convergence.
  - Assert survivor PID = tmux list-panes -t _portal-saver -F '#{pane_pid}'.
- Scenario B — clean state, zero signals:
  - Start only legitimate saver-pane daemon.
  - Capture logger output (recording bootstrap.Logger).
  - Invoke SweepOrphanDaemons().
  - Assert ZERO entries match "sweep: killed orphan daemon".
  - Assert pgrep -fxc still 1.
- Scenario C — recycled PID refusal:
  - Spawn sleep 30; via custom Pgrep seam pretend its PID in candidate set. Real IdentifyDaemon returns IdentifyNotPortalDaemon. Assert no SIGKILL sent (sleep still alive 200ms post-sweep).

Acceptance Criteria:
- Runs only under integration build tag.
- Uses portaltest.NewIsolatedStateEnv — fingerprint-diff verifies no developer state mutation.
- Uses real portal state daemon subprocesses, real tmux server, real production OrphanSweepCore.
- Scenario A converges to pgrep -fxc == 1 within 3s.
- Scenario A: survivor PID = _portal-saver pane PID.
- Scenario B: zero "sweep: killed orphan daemon" entries.
- Scenario C: non-daemon PID never SIGKILLed.
- All spawned subprocesses cleaned via t.Cleanup.

Tests:
- 3 daemons (1 saver-pane + 2 orphans) converge to pgrep -fxc == 1 after SweepOrphanDaemons
- clean steady state (only saver-pane daemon) produces zero killed-orphan log entries
- recycled-PID refusal — sleep process survives a sweep that names its PID
- cleanup ensures no leaked daemons after test exit

Edge Cases:
- Orphan subprocesses exit before sweep (segfault) → clear diagnostic; NOT silent skip.
- pgrep not on PATH (CI image without procps) → t.Skip with clear reason.
- tmuxtest _portal-saver pane process dies between setup and sweep → re-probe + retry once before failing.
- Race between Pgrep and orphan spawn — test waits for pgrep -fxc to report 3 before sweep.

Spec Reference: § Component B acceptance: given N concurrent portal state daemon processes where N-1 are orphans, SweepOrphanDaemons kills N-1. Verified by pgrep -fxc returning 1. Clean state: zero signals, no "sweep: killed orphan daemon" entries.

title: Add pre-acquire daemon.pid liveness check to AcquireDaemonLock
description:
Component C step 1. flock excludes per-inode, not per-path. If daemon.lock unlinked + recreated, two daemons flock different inodes — both succeed, singleton silently broken. Primary defence is pre-acquire daemon.pid liveness check (file content we control, sidestepping flock per-inode limitation). See phase-4-tasks.md task 4-6 for full detail.

Solution: Augment state.AcquireDaemonLock with pre-acquire check: read daemon.pid via state.ReadPIDFile, identity-check recorded PID via state.IdentifyDaemon, return ErrDaemonLockHeld if recorded PID alive AND identity-checks as portal state daemon. Absent/stale/wrong-identity PIDs fall through to existing open + flock path.

Do:
- Edit internal/state/daemon_lock.go — augment AcquireDaemonLock (lines 55-77).
- Insert pre-check BEFORE existing os.OpenFile(path, ...):
  1. state.ReadPIDFile(stateDir). On os.IsNotExist or any read error: treat as no holder, proceed.
  2. If valid PID: state.IdentifyDaemon(pid). Branch:
     - IdentifyIsPortalDaemon (err nil): return ErrDaemonLockHeld IMMEDIATELY — do not open daemon.lock.
     - IdentifyDead, IdentifyNotPortalDaemon: proceed.
     - err != nil (transient): proceed. Rationale: flock EWOULDBLOCK still catches contention; bias toward "let legitimate succession proceed" safer than spuriously blocking startup.
- Add seams: lockAcquireIdentifyDaemon = IdentifyDaemon, lockAcquireReadPIDFile = ReadPIDFile (follow existing lockAcquire seam on line 25).
- Document new pre-check in AcquireDaemonLock docstring: when ErrDaemonLockHeld via pre-check vs EWOULDBLOCK; transient → proceed rationale; layered enforcement (pre-check primary, flock EWOULDBLOCK fallback).

Acceptance Criteria:
- Pre-check runs BEFORE os.OpenFile(path, O_RDWR|O_CREATE, ...).
- daemon.pid absent → pre-check skipped, proceeds.
- daemon.pid present, recorded PID dead → proceeds.
- daemon.pid present, recorded PID alive but IdentifyNotPortalDaemon (recycled) → proceeds.
- daemon.pid present, recorded PID alive AND IdentifyIsPortalDaemon → returns ErrDaemonLockHeld WITHOUT opening daemon.lock (verified by stubbing lockAcquire to assert never called).
- daemon.pid present, identity-check transient → proceeds.
- ReadPIDFile error (non-IsNotExist) → treated as no holder, proceeds.
- Existing EWOULDBLOCK/open(2)/FD_CLOEXEC paths bytes-identical pre-task.

Tests (8):
- pre-check: daemon.pid absent → proceeds, opens daemon.lock, acquires
- pre-check: daemon.pid records dead PID → proceeds, acquires
- pre-check: daemon.pid records live PID that identity-checks as portal state daemon → returns ErrDaemonLockHeld without opening daemon.lock
- pre-check: daemon.pid records live PID that identity-checks as IdentifyNotPortalDaemon → proceeds, acquires
- pre-check: daemon.pid records live PID, identity-check returns transient err → proceeds, acquires
- pre-check: ReadPIDFile non-IsNotExist error → proceeds
- pre-check: does NOT open daemon.lock when returning ErrDaemonLockHeld
- existing EWOULDBLOCK path: pre-check finds no holder, flock fails → returns ErrDaemonLockHeld (regression)

Edge Cases:
- daemon.pid empty/malformed — ReadPIDFile parse-error behaviour; treat as no holder.
- daemon.pid records own PID — IdentifyDaemon against os.Getpid() likely returns IdentifyIsPortalDaemon if running as portal state daemon argv → ErrDaemonLockHeld. Acceptable degenerate; production never writes pidfile before lock (4-8 enforces).
- Recycled PID with coincidental "portal state daemon" argv — extreme corner; acceptable false-positive per spec.

Spec Reference: § Component C step 1 — Pre-acquire daemon.pid liveness check. Spec deviation note: pre-acquire daemon.pid check is primary singleton enforcer instead of investigation's O_EXCL|O_CREAT + fstat-vs-stat shape.

title: Add post-flock fstat-vs-stat inode cross-check with bounded retry
description:
Component C step 3. Even after 4-6 pre-check, small race window remains: between OpenFile and Flock, third party could unlink + recreate daemon.lock. Our fd references one inode while path resolves to another — we hold flock on wrong inode. Secondary defence: fstat-vs-stat post-flock with bounded retry. See phase-4-tasks.md task 4-7 for full detail.

Solution: After unix.Flock succeeds, fstat fd + stat path; compare inode numbers. Mismatch → close fd (release flock), sleep 10ms, retry full acquire up to 3 attempts. Persistent mismatch → return wrapped error (NOT ErrDaemonLockHeld — daemon-internal status-1-exit path).

Do:
- Edit internal/state/daemon_lock.go — augment AcquireDaemonLock.
- Restructure into outer loop bounded by 3 attempts:
  1. Pre-check (4-6) — ErrDaemonLockHeld returns immediately, no retry.
  2. Existing os.OpenFile(path, O_RDWR|O_CREATE, 0o600).
  3. Existing lockAcquire(int(f.Fd()), LOCK_EX|LOCK_NB) — EWOULDBLOCK returns ErrDaemonLockHeld (existing behaviour, no retry).
  4. NEW inode cross-check:
     - var fdStat unix.Stat_t; unix.Fstat(int(f.Fd()), &fdStat) → fdInode := fdStat.Ino.
     - var pathStat unix.Stat_t; unix.Stat(path, &pathStat) → pathInode := pathStat.Ino.
     - On fstat/stat syscall error: close fd, return wrapped error immediately (do NOT retry — structural).
     - If fdInode == pathInode: lock acquired, run existing FD_CLOEXEC, return (f, nil).
     - If fdInode != pathInode: close fd (release flock), time.Sleep(10*time.Millisecond), increment attempt counter, continue outer loop.
  5. Existing unix.FcntlInt(..., FD_CLOEXEC) runs only after inode cross-check passes.
- On exit with attempts exhausted (3 mismatches): return wrapped error ("daemon.lock inode mismatch after 3 attempts: fd inode != path inode"). NOT ErrDaemonLockHeld. Callers must NOT match via errors.Is(err, ErrDaemonLockHeld). defaultDaemonRun treats wrapped errors as ERROR-and-exit-non-zero (status 1 with WARN per spec).
- Add seams: lockAcquireFstat = unix.Fstat, lockAcquireStat = unix.Stat. Reset via t.Cleanup.
- Bound retry: total worst-case < 100ms (3 attempts × 10ms + negligible syscalls ≈ 30ms). Fixed 10ms sleep; no jitter (spec specifies fixed).

Acceptance Criteria:
- fstat(fd).Ino == stat(path).Ino verified after every successful flock.
- Mismatch attempt 1, match attempt 2 → succeed.
- Mismatch attempts 1,2,3 → wrapped error after attempt 3. Total wall-time < 100ms.
- Fstat/Stat syscall error → close fd, wrapped error immediately, no retry.
- Match attempt 1 (happy path): no sleep, no retry, no delay.
- EWOULDBLOCK: returns ErrDaemonLockHeld from inside first attempt's flock — no retry, no inode check.
- Pre-check ErrDaemonLockHeld: returns immediately, no retry.
- FD_CLOEXEC only after inode cross-check passes.
- Wrapped error NOT matched by errors.Is(err, ErrDaemonLockHeld) — callers distinguish.

Tests (10):
- inode-check happy path: fd inode == path inode on first attempt
- mismatch attempt 1, match attempt 2 succeeds
- mismatch attempts 1,2,3 → wrapped non-ErrDaemonLockHeld error within 100ms
- Fstat syscall error → wrapped error immediately, no retry
- Stat syscall error → wrapped error immediately, no retry
- mismatch closes fd before retry (recorded close-call count)
- FD_CLOEXEC not called when inode mismatch persistent
- bounded retry total wall-time < 100ms (measured)
- regression EWOULDBLOCK: returns ErrDaemonLockHeld via existing flock failure, inode check not reached
- regression pre-check: ErrDaemonLockHeld skips inode check entirely

Edge Cases:
- File replaced between attempts by fast competitor — retry observes new inode; if stabilises, attempt 2 or 3 succeeds.
- File deleted by competitor between flock and fstat — fstat on fd returns original inode (fd holds reference). stat on path returns ENOENT — syscall error → immediate wrapped-error return.
- Two daemons in lockstep — both retry up to 3 times; at most one wins per attempt; loser falls to pre-check or EWOULDBLOCK next attempt.

Spec Reference: § Component C step 3 — Post-flock inode cross-check. Bounded 3 retries, 10ms sleep. Persistent mismatch returns wrapped error. Exit: defaultDaemonRun treats wrapped error as ERROR + exit status 1 with WARN. Distinct from ErrDaemonLockHeld (status 0). destroy-unattached=off prevents restart loop (F).

title: AST-walking test asserts WritePIDFile immediately follows acquireDaemonLock in defaultDaemonRun
description:
Component C correctness depends on window between AcquireDaemonLock returning and WritePIDFile completing being bounded by single function call — implementers MUST NOT insert other work between them. Code-review-only enforcement fragile. Spec explicitly requires AST-walking unit test. See phase-4-tasks.md task 4-8 for full detail.

Solution: Unit test parses cmd/state_daemon.go via go/parser + go/ast, locates defaultDaemonRun, identifies assignment with RHS call to acquireDaemonLock, asserts next statement (allowing guarded-equivalent if err != nil { return } sandwich) contains call to state.WritePIDFile. Test fails if any new statement intrudes.

Do:
- Add cmd/state_daemon_lock_pid_ordering_test.go (no integration build tag — pure source-walking unit test).
- Test:
  1. os.ReadFile("state_daemon.go") (relative to test package directory).
  2. go/parser.ParseFile(fset, "state_daemon.go", src, parser.ParseComments).
  3. ast.Inspect to find *ast.FuncDecl with Name == defaultDaemonRun.
  4. Walk Body.List to find index i where Body.List[i] is *ast.AssignStmt with RHS *ast.CallExpr to identifier acquireDaemonLock.
  5. Assert Body.List[i+1] is *ast.IfStmt matching if err != nil { ... return ... } shape (guarded-equivalent permitted).
  6. Assert Body.List[i+2] is *ast.IfStmt whose body contains call to state.WritePIDFile. Pattern: if err := state.WritePIDFile(dir, os.Getpid()); err != nil { return ... }.
  7. Allow comments between statements (don't appear in Body.List).
  8. Fail with precise diagnostic naming intruding statement type if Body.List[i+2] is anything other than expected WritePIDFile call.
- Document in preamble: spec contract is "next call statement after successful acquireDaemonLock is WritePIDFile", with if err != nil { return } guards permitted.
- Test verifies defaultDaemonRun exists in expected file; if function moved/renamed, test fails fast with "function not found" rather than silently passing.
- Companion assertion: AcquireDaemonLock (public) has exactly one production call site — walk cmd/ via go/packages or os.ReadFile-and-string-scan. Spec: "No other production call site of AcquireDaemonLock exists". Defence against future caller without ordering check.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Compiles + runs as normal unit test (no integration build tag).
- Parses cmd/state_daemon.go via go/parser; locates defaultDaemonRun via AST walk.
- Asserts statement following acquireDaemonLock matches if err != nil { return } guarded shape.
- Asserts next statement after guard is state.WritePIDFile call (wrapped in own if err != nil { return } guard).
- If any other statement inserted between lock-acquire and pid-write, fails with diagnostic naming intruding statement AST type + line number.
- Companion: AcquireDaemonLock has exactly one production call site; test fails if second added.
- Passes against current source.

Tests:
- AST ordering: acquireDaemonLock immediately followed by err-guard and then state.WritePIDFile in defaultDaemonRun
- AST ordering: clear diagnostic naming intruding statement when synthetic mutation injects unrelated statement (meta-test)
- AST ordering: AcquireDaemonLock has exactly one production call site in cmd/

Edge Cases:
- Function renamed from defaultDaemonRun — fails with "function not found" rather than silent pass.
- state package prefix dropped (WritePIDFile vs state.WritePIDFile) — match either via name-only check on call's function expression.
- Comments between calls — don't appear in Body.List, inherently permitted.
- Future refactor introduces acquireDaemonLock in other functions (e.g., recoverLock helper) — companion call-site count assertion catches.
- Lock-acquire wrapped in different control-flow (moved into helper) — AST walk doesn't find inside defaultDaemonRun → fails with "lock acquire not found in defaultDaemonRun body".

Spec Reference: § Component C step 4 — Post-acquire daemon.pid write. Daemon writes daemon.pid as next statement after successful acquireDaemonLock in cmd/state_daemon.go defaultDaemonRun. No other production call site exists; spec contract is production daemon's defaultDaemonRun only.

title: Integration test for Component C upgrade-path two-binary scenario
description:
Component C must handle real-world upgrade landmine: v(N) portal state daemon running + holds lock; user upgrades to v(N+1) and invokes portal open. New binary's bootstrap spawns own daemon — A+B sweep prior daemon, so by time new daemon calls AcquireDaemonLock, prior daemon.pid is stale. Spec mandates integration test for two-binary scenario. See phase-4-tasks.md task 4-9 for full detail.

Solution: Integration test builds two distinct portal binaries (treated as v(N) and v(N+1)), spawns v(N) daemon, invokes v(N+1) bootstrap, asserts converged end state: exactly one portal state daemon survives, fresh-process AcquireDaemonLock from test refuses with ErrDaemonLockHeld.

Do:
- Place in internal/state/daemon_lock_upgrade_integration_test.go (or cmd/bootstrap/) under integration build tag.
- Use portaltest.NewIsolatedStateEnv(t).
- Use portalbintest.BuildPortalBinary. Two binaries:
  - Option A (preferred): build same source twice into distinct paths (portalA, portalB) — exec from different inodes.
  - Option B: build once, mark daemons by env var (PORTAL_DAEMON_TAG=A vs B).
- Use tmuxtest for real tmux server. Create _portal-saver with placeholder pane (post-Phase-3 ordering — tail -f /dev/null), set destroy-unattached=off, respawn-pane -k to launch v(N) daemon as pane.
- Wait until v(N) writes daemon.pid (poll 3s).
- Invoke v(N+1) bootstrap. Two equivalent forms:
  - Form A: portal open subprocess against same tmux + isolated state dir.
  - Form B: construct production Orchestrator directly from test (via internal/bootstrapadapter.NewProductionOrchestrator), call Run. Cleaner test surface.
- Assert convergence:
  - pgrep -fxc 'portal state daemon' returns 1 within 6s.
  - Survivor PID is NOT v(N) PID — A/B swept it; new saver-pane daemon is survivor.
  - daemon.pid on disk references survivor.
- Assert Component C pre-check from third process: invoke state.AcquireDaemonLock(stateDir) from test goroutine. Must return state.ErrDaemonLockHeld.
- Alternative: also exercise C in isolation (Sweep disabled via seam). v(N) survives bootstrap window; v(N+1) AcquireDaemonLock refuses cleanly via pre-check — no destructive coexistence.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Runs only under integration build tag.
- Uses portaltest.NewIsolatedStateEnv + portalbintest.BuildPortalBinary.
- Scenario A full A+B+C composition: v(N) swept, v(N+1) acquires cleanly, pgrep -fxc == 1 within 6s.
- Scenario B C-in-isolation (Sweep disabled): v(N) survives, v(N+1) AcquireDaemonLock refuses via pre-check, no destructive coexistence.
- Test-side state.AcquireDaemonLock from fresh goroutine after bootstrap returns ErrDaemonLockHeld.
- All spawned daemons cleaned via t.Cleanup.

Tests:
- v(N) running, v(N+1) bootstrap converges to single daemon via A+B+C composition
- C in isolation (Sweep disabled) — v(N+1) refuses cleanly via pre-check, no destructive coexistence
- post-bootstrap fresh-process AcquireDaemonLock returns ErrDaemonLockHeld

Edge Cases:
- v(N) exits between bootstrap entry and AcquireDaemonLock (A escalation succeeds, daemon dies before C pre-check) — pre-check sees IdentifyDead, proceeds, succeeds. Legitimate-succession case; test must not fail.
- v(N) daemon.pid stale (daemon exited earlier without cleanup) — pre-check sees dead PID, proceeds. Test exercises.
- A/B disabled in scenario B — wire orchestrator with no-op Sweep + no-op Escalation seams; leave C live. Confirm via directed test-deps construction.
- pgrep not on PATH — t.Skip with clear reason.

Spec Reference: § Component C acceptance — Upgrade-path scenario: spawn v(N) daemon holding lock, invoke v(N+1) binary bootstrap. With A+B+C, new bootstrap's daemon either acquires cleanly (A/B swept prior + daemon.pid no longer live) or refuses cleanly via pre-check (no destructive coexistence). Verified by integration test.

title: Composite integration test: A+B+C converge to pgrep -fxc == 1 within 6 s
description:
Tasks 4-1 through 4-9 prove components individually. Spec Phase 4 acceptance calls for end-to-end composition: given reporter's failure scenario (1 legitimate + 2 orphans, one with daemon.pid reference + one without), post-bootstrap end state must be exactly one live portal state daemon within 6s, scrollback stable across 10 consecutive 1s observations, Component C pre-check verifiable from fresh process. Phase-internal composition test (Phase 6 provides broader composite incorporating D/E/F). See phase-4-tasks.md task 4-10 for full detail.

Solution: Integration test reconstructs three-daemon reporter scenario, invokes production bootstrap orchestrator with A+B+C active, asserts converged end state across pgrep, scrollback stability, fresh-process AcquireDaemonLock refusal.

Do:
- Place in cmd/bootstrap/composition_abc_integration_test.go (or internal/restoretest/) under integration build tag.
- Use portaltest.NewIsolatedStateEnv(t).
- Use portalbintest.BuildPortalBinary.
- Use tmuxtest for real tmux server with _portal-saver + 2 user sessions.
- Setup:
  1. Create _portal-saver with legitimate daemon as pane (post-Phase-3 ordering — placeholder → destroy-unattached=off → respawn to portal state daemon).
  2. Wait until legitimate daemon writes daemon.pid.
  3. Spawn 2 orphan daemons against same stateDir, isolated env. Orphan 1: starts after legitimate, overwrites daemon.pid with own PID (simulating "orphan with daemon.pid reference" reporter case). Orphan 2: starts but does NOT write daemon.pid — only visible via pgrep. Both are direct test subprocesses (not tmux pane processes); parent is test, NOT saver pane.
  4. Assert pre-state: pgrep -fxc == 3.
- Bootstrap:
  5. Invoke production Orchestrator.Run directly (preferred) or portal open subprocess. Start timer at orchestrator entry.
- Post-bootstrap:
  6. Poll pgrep -fxc until 1, bounded 6s from orchestrator entry. 6s = A escalation budget (5s session-kill + 1s SIGKILL) + B sweep latency (sub-second).
  7. Survivor PID = _portal-saver pane PID (saver-pane daemon respawned by F during bootstrap step 5, OR originally-spawned saver-pane daemon — either acceptable, same post-state).
  8. Scrollback stability: every 1s for 10 observations, snapshot <stateDir>/scrollback/ (path-keyed fingerprint map). Assert every observation byte-identical to first — no .bin deletions, no unexpected new files, no size/mtime changes.
  9. Fresh-process Component C pre-check: from test goroutine, state.AcquireDaemonLock(stateDir). Assert returns state.ErrDaemonLockHeld.
- Cleanup:
  10. t.Cleanup kills all daemon processes, tears down tmux, runs fingerprint-diff against developer real state dir (Phase 1 helper).

Acceptance Criteria:
- Runs only under integration build tag.
- Uses portaltest.NewIsolatedStateEnv + portalbintest.BuildPortalBinary.
- Pre-state: pgrep -fxc 'portal state daemon' == 3 before bootstrap.
- Post-bootstrap: pgrep -fxc == 1 within 6s.
- Survivor PID = _portal-saver pane PID via tmux list-panes -t _portal-saver -F '#{pane_pid}'.
- Scrollback stability: 10 consecutive 1s snapshots byte-identical; any delta fails with path-and-field diagnostic (reuse fingerprint diff from 4-2).
- Fresh-process state.AcquireDaemonLock(stateDir) returns state.ErrDaemonLockHeld.
- All spawned subprocesses cleaned via t.Cleanup.
- Test fails (not silently passes) if pgrep unavailable — t.Skip with clear message.

Tests:
- composition A+B+C: 3 daemons converge to 1 within 6 s after orchestrator entry
- composition A+B+C: scrollback directory stable across 10×1 s post-bootstrap observations
- composition A+B+C: fresh-process AcquireDaemonLock refuses with ErrDaemonLockHeld via Component C pre-check
- composition A+B+C: survivor is the saver-pane daemon, not one of the orphans

Edge Cases:
- One orphan exits naturally before bootstrap (crashes) — pre-state catches; fails clearly rather than silent N=2.
- Legitimate daemon dies during bootstrap (A escalation kills wrong PID) — survivor mismatch catches; fails with actual vs expected.
- Scrollback dir empty at start of 10×1s window — must seed activity (user pane with non-trivial output); empty-then-empty pair not informative.
- 6s budget exceeded by small margin (6.5s) — fails with measured time; do NOT add slack without spec update.
- Subprocess cleanup leaves zombies — t.Cleanup must kill -9 + wait for reap.
- Composition with Phase 3 (F) — assumes Phase 3 shipped; saver creation uses placeholder-then-respawn. Document phase dependency in preamble.

Spec Reference: § Composite End-to-End Verification (Phase-4-scoped subset). Broader composite in Phase 6 incorporates D/E/F. This 4-10 covers A+B+C subset. 6s budget = A escalation budget (5s + 1s) + B sub-second sweep latency.

title: Measure legitimate transient durations and lock in selfSupervisionHysteresisTicks with in-source provenance
description:
Component D hysteresis N is the only tuning knob. Risk Summary marks empirical measurement as REQUIRED mitigation. See phase-5-tasks.md task 5-1 for full detail.

Solution: Run measurement harness against real tmux server (tmuxtest) exercising four scenarios — steady-state, attach/detach cycles, client-attached hook, bootstrap kill-and-recreate — and records for each scenario max consecutive ticks during which hypothetical self-check would have observed failing saver-membership. N = clamp(ceil(max × 2), 3, 9). Persist memo at .workflows/.../component-d-hysteresis-measurement.md capturing per-scenario raw numbers, binary version, measurement date, tmux version, OS.

Do:
- Author measurement harness (go test with existing integration build tag, e.g., cmd/state_daemon_hysteresis_measurement_test.go).
- Harness spawns real portal state daemon subprocess (portalbintest.BuildPortalBinary + portaltest.NewIsolatedStateEnv) per scenario; records at granularity of TickerPeriod (1s) how many consecutive ticks observe saver-membership failure. Polling probe mirrors saverMembershipProbe (Task 5-2): tmux has-session -t _portal-saver + tmux list-panes -t _portal-saver -F '#{pane_pid}' compared against daemon subprocess's PID.
- Scenarios:
  1. Steady-state: daemon in healthy _portal-saver ≥30s, no interaction. Expected ≈0 ticks.
  2. Attach/detach: attach + detach repeatedly while daemon runs. Expected ≈0 ticks.
  3. client-attached hook: trigger by attaching client. Expected = transient hook command duration.
  4. Bootstrap kill-and-recreate: BootstrapPortalSaver unhealthy-saver recreate (composes with Phase 3).
- Write per-scenario raw measurements (min/max/median across ≥5 runs) to memo. Include tmux -V, uname -srm, binary version (cmd.version ldflag), date.
- Compute N = clamp(ceil(max_observed × 2), 3, 9). If max × 2 > 5 flag in memo as "evidence of upstream defect" per Risk Summary; still pick clamped value.
- Add constant in cmd/state_daemon.go near top (above defaultDaemonRun):
  // selfSupervisionHysteresisTicks is Component D's consecutive-failing-tick threshold.
  // Measured worst-case transients (date YYYY-MM-DD, binary vX.Y.Z, tmux V):
  //   steady-state:           N1 ticks
  //   attach/detach:          N2 ticks
  //   client-attached hook:   N3 ticks
  //   bootstrap kill/recreate:N4 ticks
  // 2x safety factor applied; clamped to single-digit ticks.
  // Memo: .workflows/.../component-d-hysteresis-measurement.md
  const selfSupervisionHysteresisTicks = N
- No runtime override (env var or flag) — compile-time per spec; Task 5-9's unit test guards lower bound.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Constant selfSupervisionHysteresisTicks exists in cmd/state_daemon.go as const (literal, not var).
- Comment block names all four scenarios with measured tick counts, 2x safety factor, date, binary version, memo path.
- Memo committed at .workflows/.../component-d-hysteresis-measurement.md with per-scenario raw numbers, tmux version, OS, binary version, date.
- Value satisfies 3 ≤ N ≤ 9.
- If max × 2 > 5: memo flags evidence of upstream defect.
- Harness uses portaltest.NewIsolatedStateEnv + portalbintest.BuildPortalBinary + tmuxtest (no mocks for tmux or daemon).

Tests:
- Harness doubles as re-runnable verification artifact. Emits scenario worst-case values; asserts each ≤ N/2 (safety-factor invariant). Re-running against future regression fails loudly.
- No separate unit test in this task — lower-bound guard owned by Task 5-9.

Edge Cases:
- Scenario 3 bounded by portal state signal-hydrate latency — record raw values even if zero.
- Scenario 4 measures gap during bootstrap kill-and-recreate; with Phase 3 should be <1 tick but record.
- CI without interactive tmux: scenario 2 simulate via tmux attach -d from parallel goroutine; document in memo.
- Future implementer re-runs and gets larger worst-case — MUST update memo + in-source comment in same commit.

Spec Reference: § Component D — "Hysteresis N: 3 consecutive ticks" rationale + "Measurement artefact for N". § Risk Summary: empirical measurement MUST happen. Single-digit ticks ceiling; >5 = upstream defect.

title: Extract saverMembershipProbe seam and add tmux.SaverPanePID helper
description:
Component D self-check needs to call tmux has-session -t _portal-saver + tmux list-panes -F '#{pane_pid}' every tick and compare pane pid to os.Getpid(). Existing *tmux.Client exposes HasSession but no helper for fetching single-pane pid. Without seam, unit test for counter-reset (5-4) cannot stub membership signal without hard dependency. See phase-5-tasks.md task 5-2 for full detail.

Solution: Two artefacts:
1. tmux.SaverPanePID(c *Client, sessionName string) (int, error) in internal/tmux/ — thin helper shelling out to tmux list-panes -t {sessionName} -F '#{pane_pid}', parses first line, returns int pid. Classifies errors: no such session → wraps tmux.ErrNoSuchSession (Phase 2); empty output → ErrEmptyPaneList sentinel; parse failure → ErrPanePIDParse sentinel.
2. saverMembershipProbe seam in cmd/state_daemon.go — package-level func var with signature func(c *tmux.Client, selfPID int) bool. Production wires to defaultSaverMembershipProbe calling c.HasSession(tmux.PortalSaverName) and tmux.SaverPanePID(c, tmux.PortalSaverName). Tests overwrite via t.Cleanup to inject deterministic absent/present/mismatch sequences.

Do:
- Add tmux.SaverPanePID in internal/tmux/portal_saver.go (or new internal/tmux/saver_pane_pid.go).
  - Impl: c.commander.Run("tmux", "list-panes", "-t", "="+sessionName, "-F", "#{pane_pid}") (use existing =-prefix exact-match convention).
  - Parse first line via strconv.Atoi.
  - If stderr contains "no such session" wrap with tmux.ErrNoSuchSession (Phase 2).
  - If stdout empty: return wrapped ErrEmptyPaneList (new).
  - If parse fails: return wrapped ErrPanePIDParse (new).
  - Multi-line stdout: take first non-empty line; defensively log via caller.
- Add saverMembershipProbe to cmd/state_daemon.go near other seam vars (daemonRunFunc, daemonShutdownFunc, acquireDaemonLock):
  var saverMembershipProbe = defaultSaverMembershipProbe
  func defaultSaverMembershipProbe(c *tmux.Client, selfPID int) bool {
      if !c.HasSession(tmux.PortalSaverName) { return false }
      pid, err := tmux.SaverPanePID(c, tmux.PortalSaverName)
      if err != nil { return false }
      return pid == selfPID
  }
  Any tmux error (including race-induced ErrNoSuchSession) → "absent" per spec ("Treat any error (not just session-not-found) as absent").
- Do NOT integrate probe into tick loop in this task — Task 5-3 owns integration.

Acceptance Criteria:
- tmux.SaverPanePID(*Client, string) (int, error) exists with documented error classification.
- tmux.SaverPanePID unit tests use existing Commander mock pattern; cover: success, no-such-session-wraps-ErrNoSuchSession, empty-pane-list, parse-failure, multi-line-takes-first, generic-exec-error.
- saverMembershipProbe seam exists in cmd/state_daemon.go; production wires to defaultSaverMembershipProbe.
- defaultSaverMembershipProbe returns false on any error path (not just no-such-session).
- Returns true only when HasSession true AND SaverPanePID returns pid == selfPID with no error.
- No call to probe from tick loop in this task.

Tests:
- internal/tmux/saver_pane_pid_test.go: 6 cases (success, ErrNoSuchSession, ErrEmptyPaneList, ErrPanePIDParse, multi-line first, generic exec error)
- cmd/state_daemon_test.go: 4 cases (HasSession false, SaverPanePID errors, pid matches selfPID, pid != selfPID)

Edge Cases:
- HasSession true but session destroyed before SaverPanePID — ErrNoSuchSession treated as absent.
- Multi-line list-panes output (defensive — _portal-saver should have one pane) — first non-empty line.
- strconv.Atoi("") on whitespace-only — ErrPanePIDParse.
- No t.Parallel() per CLAUDE.md.

Spec Reference: § Component D self-check sequence steps 1-3. Phase 2 sentinel tmux.ErrNoSuchSession reused. =<session> exact-match convention from internal/tmux/tmux.go:335.

title: Integrate per-tick self-check before captureAndCommit with os.Exit(0) eject
description:
Daemon ticks forever after acquiring daemon.lock until SIGHUP or context cancellation. No per-tick check that daemon is still bound to live _portal-saver pane. Between bootstraps orphan daemons accumulate freely — reporter had 13-hour orphan lifetime. Without in-loop self-eject, A+B only address orphans at bootstrap, leaving inter-bootstrap window uncovered. See phase-5-tasks.md task 5-3 for full detail.

Solution: Insert saver-membership self-check in ticker for-loop of defaultDaemonRun (NOT inside tick), in case <-ticker.C arm BEFORE call to tick(ctx, deps). Satisfies spec ordering (before captureAndCommit) AND ensures self-check is not skipped by any early-return inside tick (notably IsRestoringSet). Maintain consecutive-failing-tick counter as closure-scoped var in defaultDaemonRun before ticker loop. On match: reset to 0. On miss: increment. When counter reaches selfSupervisionHysteresisTicks: log INFO, call os.Exit(0). os.Exit(0) skips deferred handlers — defaultShutdownFlush does NOT run. Stale daemon.pid left in place (Phase 4 Component C pre-check handles on next acquire).

Do:
- Edit cmd/state_daemon.go:
  - Self-check lives in ticker for-loop of defaultDaemonRun, NOT inside tick. Place in case <-ticker.C arm BEFORE tick(ctx, deps) call. Satisfies spec ordering (captureAndCommit runs inside tick) AND ensures self-check is not skipped by IsRestoringSet short-circuit.
  - Consecutive-failing-tick counter is closure-scoped var inside defaultDaemonRun, declared immediately before ticker for-loop. Resets on probe-true; never persisted across daemon process lifetimes.
  - Add seam: var osExit = os.Exit. Tests overwrite via t.Cleanup.
  - Add Info method on state.Logger if doesn't exist (inspect internal/state/logger.go — if only Warn/Error, add Info sibling). Spec uses INFO explicitly.
  - Pseudo-code (canonical):
    var consecutiveAbsenceTicks int
    for {
        select {
        case <-ticker.C:
            if saverMembershipProbe(deps.Client, os.Getpid()) {
                consecutiveAbsenceTicks = 0
            } else {
                consecutiveAbsenceTicks++
                if consecutiveAbsenceTicks >= selfSupervisionHysteresisTicks {
                    deps.Logger.Info(state.ComponentDaemon,
                        "self-supervision: saver-membership lost for %d consecutive ticks, exiting",
                        consecutiveAbsenceTicks)
                    osExit(0)
                }
            }
            tick(ctx, deps)
        case <-ctx.Done():
            return daemonShutdownFunc(deps)
        }
    }
  - Code comment above self-check citing: spec ordering (before captureAndCommit, satisfied by placement before tick call in ticker arm); why NOT inside tick (any early-return inside tick e.g. IsRestoringSet would mask divergent orphan during restore); why os.Exit(0) not graceful (bypasses daemonShutdownFunc defers); why daemon.pid NOT deleted (C pre-check handles stale; deleting would be racy + invert layered-enforcement).

Acceptance Criteria:
- Self-check runs in ticker for-loop arm BEFORE tick(ctx, deps), called exactly once per tick.
- Counter resets to 0 on every probe-true (not decrement).
- On tick where counter reaches N: INFO log under state.ComponentDaemon emitted with literal "self-supervision: saver-membership lost for" + consecutive count.
- Immediately after INFO log: osExit(0) seam called.
- No deferred handler runs on eject — daemonShutdownFunc / defaultShutdownFlush NOT invoked.
- daemon.pid NOT deleted before eject.
- state.Logger.Info exists (added if needed).
- N=3: three consecutive false returns trigger eject; two false then one true resets counter.

Tests (in cmd/state_daemon_self_supervision_test.go):
- self-check runs before IsRestoringSet — eject fires even when @portal-restoring set
- self-check runs before captureAndCommit — counter increments do not trigger captureAndCommit calls
- eject calls osExit(0) and stops further tick body execution
- eject does not call daemonShutdownFunc (recorded via swapped seam)
- eject does not delete daemon.pid (assert file still exists post-eject)
- counter increments only on probe-false; probe-true resets to 0 every time

Edge Cases:
- Self-check during @portal-restoring window: legitimate stays 0 (probe returns true); orphans don't gain immunity.
- selfSupervisionHysteresisTicks == 1 (lower bound): single failing tick ejects.
- Probe false for k < N then true: counter resets cleanly, no eject (5-4 covers).
- osExit(0) skipping daemon.pid removal is INTENTIONAL.
- Logger Info method may not exist — adding in scope.

Spec Reference: § Component D self-check sequence steps 4-5.

title: Unit test counter reset on transient-then-recover via stubbed probe
description:
Legitimate daemon must not exit on single transient tmux hiccup. Spec rejects N=1 — one-tick hiccup would unnecessarily kill daemon. Counter must zero FULLY on first recovered tick, not decrement. Buggy implementation (counter-- instead of counter=0) would pass naive eject test but fail transient-then-recover. See phase-5-tasks.md task 5-4 for full detail.

Solution: Unit test overrides saverMembershipProbe with scripted sequence: returns false for k < N consecutive ticks, then true, then continues. Drives defaultDaemonRun through enough ticks to exercise multiple absent-then-present cycles; asserts (a) osExit never called, (b) counter behavior consistent with full reset, (c) daemon survives arbitrarily long under repeated near-miss cycles.

Do:
- Place test in cmd/state_daemon_self_supervision_test.go (or alongside 5-3 tests).
- Use fast TickerPeriod (e.g., 1ms — matches precedent at cmd/state_daemon_run_test.go:195).
- Probe stub backed by chan bool or indexed []bool. Test scripts:
  - For round in [0, 5):
    - Push N-1 false returns.
    - Push 1 true.
