  - Then push 100 trues for steady state verification.
- Override osExit to record call + panic the test (any eject = immediate failure).
- Override daemonShutdownFunc to no-op so test drives ticks past survival window.
- Run defaultDaemonRun in goroutine; cancel context after 5*N + 100 ticks worth of wall time; assert osExit never called.
- Boundary case k = N-1 exactly — N-1 falses then true must not eject across many cycles.
- Probe returning transient errors counted as absent: since seam signature is bool (not (bool, error)), test asserts seam returns false + verifies counter increments. Mapping tmux error → false owned by default impl in 5-2.

Acceptance Criteria:
- No t.Parallel() (CLAUDE.md).
- Exercises ≥5 absent-then-recover cycles with k = N-1; asserts osExit never invoked.
- Exercises exact boundary k = N-1 (not just k < N-1).
- Fast TickerPeriod, runtime <~200ms total.
- All seams (saverMembershipProbe, osExit, daemonShutdownFunc) restored via t.Cleanup.
- Verifies: after single true return, next N-1 false returns also do not eject — proving full reset not decrement.

Tests:
- counter resets fully on first probe-true after k<N absent ticks
- counter resets at exact boundary k=N-1 absent then 1 present
- counter resets across many absent-present cycles, daemon never exits
- counter increments uniformly on probe-false regardless of cause

Edge Cases:
- N=1 trivial — lower-bound guard (5-9) forbids zero, production N≥3 from 5-1. Parameterize test on selfSupervisionHysteresisTicks (read at setup) so N bump doesn't require rewrites.
- Probe stub drained safely if daemon ticks faster than scripted entries — pad generously, fail loudly on underrun.
- daemonShutdownFunc swap must restore production default to avoid leaking into sibling tests.

Spec Reference: § Component D acceptance: "No false-positive exit on legitimate transient." Stub returns absent for k<N then present: daemon does NOT exit, counter resets. § Hysteresis rationale: N=1 rejected because single tmux-command hiccup would unnecessarily kill legitimate daemon.

title: Integration test self-eject when _portal-saver absent
description:
Unit tests with stubbed probes prove counter logic but cannot prove production defaultSaverMembershipProbe wired to real tmux drives the eject. First integration acceptance — Self-eject on absent saver — requires spawning real portal state daemon subprocess against real tmux server WITHOUT _portal-saver and observing exit within (N+1) * TickerPeriod. See phase-5-tasks.md task 5-5 for full detail.

Solution: Integration test using portaltest.NewIsolatedStateEnv + portalbintest.BuildPortalBinary + tmuxtest with NO _portal-saver session. Stage stateDir with no daemon.pid (Phase 4 Component C pre-check skips + proceeds). Spawn daemon binary directly (bypass orchestrator so Component B sweep does not preempt). Assert subprocess exits within (N+1) * TickerPeriod + 2s slack.

Do:
- Place under integration build tag: cmd/state_daemon_self_supervision_integration_test.go.
- Setup:
  1. env, stateDir := portaltest.NewIsolatedStateEnv(t) — Phase 1 helper.
  2. binPath := portalbintest.BuildPortalBinary(t).
  3. Real tmux server via tmuxtest with custom socket. Do NOT create _portal-saver.
  4. Verify staging satisfies Phase 4 Component C pre-check: <stateDir>/daemon.pid does NOT exist; <stateDir>/daemon.lock does NOT exist.
  5. cmd := exec.Command(binPath, "state", "daemon"); cmd.Env = env; set TMUX env to test socket so daemon's tmux.DefaultClient() hits test server.
- Action: start subprocess; wait for exit (expected via os.Exit(0) — code 0, no defaultShutdownFlush, no stderr stack trace).
- Assertion:
  - Exit within (N+1) * 1s + 2s slack.
  - Exit code 0.
  - Daemon log file (<stateDir>/daemon.log or NewLogger path — verify) contains INFO substring "self-supervision: saver-membership lost for".
  - <stateDir>/daemon.pid either still exists with dead PID, or never existed. Assert if present: contents = subprocess's PID. Do NOT assert deletion.
- Teardown: kill tmux test server; portaltest t.Cleanup runs backstop automatically.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Tagged with integration build tag.
- Uses portaltest.NewIsolatedStateEnv — no developer state mutations.
- Stages <stateDir>/daemon.pid as absent so C pre-check proceeds.
- Spawns daemon directly (not via portal open / orchestrator) so B sweep does not preempt.
- Does NOT create _portal-saver.
- Daemon exits within (N+1) * TickerPeriod + 2s with code 0.
- Log contains "self-supervision: saver-membership lost for".
- daemon.pid if present post-exit not deleted — assert presence with stale PID, never assert deletion.

Tests:
- self-eject: portal state daemon exits within (N+1) ticks when _portal-saver absent
- self-eject: exit code 0, no panic/error stack on stderr
- self-eject: daemon.pid intentionally not deleted before exit
- self-eject: daemon log records self-supervision INFO line

Edge Cases:
- Daemon binary race: spawn → write daemon.pid → first tick → probe-false → counter=1 → ... → counter=N → osExit(0). TickerPeriod 1s, wall time to eject bounded but not instant. 2s slack on top absorbs.
- Must NOT call tmux kill-server until after subprocess exited + waited.
- Confirm daemon honours TMUX env or tmux.DefaultClient discovery — TMUX_TMPDIR or similar may be needed.

Spec Reference: § Component D acceptance: Self-eject on absent saver. § Test staging note: tests intentionally violate saver-pane-process invariant; stage state dir with no daemon.pid OR known-dead PID; spawn daemon directly bypassing orchestrator.

title: Integration test self-eject on saver pane pid mismatch via respawn-pane -k
description:
Saver-absent case (5-5) is one of two trigger conditions. Second — more realistic real-world orphan trigger — is _portal-saver exists but pane process replaced (e.g., by previous bootstrap's recreate). Daemon's PID no longer matches pane_pid. Without explicit integration test for pid-mismatch, regression confusing "session exists" with "I am still pane process" could slip past absent-saver test. See phase-5-tasks.md task 5-6 for full detail.

Solution: Integration test creates _portal-saver on real tmux with daemon binary as initial pane command. Stage <stateDir>/daemon.pid with known-dead PID (Phase 4 Component C pre-check proceeds + new daemon acquires lock). Spawn daemon subprocess (bypass orchestrator). Externally replace saver pane process via tmux respawn-pane -k -t _portal-saver 'sh -c "exec tail -f /dev/null"'. Observe daemon subprocess exit within (N+1) * TickerPeriod.

Do:
- Place alongside 5-5 in cmd/state_daemon_self_supervision_integration_test.go.
- Setup:
  1. env, stateDir := portaltest.NewIsolatedStateEnv(t).
  2. binPath := portalbintest.BuildPortalBinary(t).
  3. Real tmux server via tmuxtest custom socket.
  4. Stage <stateDir>/daemon.pid with known-dead PID: spawn short-lived process (exec.Command("true"); cmd.Run()), capture PID after Run returns (reaped), write to daemon.pid via state.WritePIDFile. IdentifyDaemon resolves as IdentifyDead so C pre-check proceeds.
  5. Create _portal-saver directly: tmux new-session -d -s _portal-saver 'sh -c "exec tail -f /dev/null"' (NOT daemon binary). Set destroy-unattached=off immediately.
- Spawn daemon:
  - cmd := exec.Command(binPath, "state", "daemon"); cmd.Env = env.
  - Wait until daemon acquires lock (poll daemon.pid contents = subprocess PID, ~2s bound).
  - Verify: daemon's PID ≠ _portal-saver pane PID (via tmux list-panes -F '#{pane_pid}'). Structural divergence detected.
- Action: wait one tick (~1s) for first self-check; optionally respawn-pane mid-test to ensure mismatch persists across N ticks.
- Assertion:
  - Exit within (N+1) * 1s + 2s slack, exit code 0.
  - Daemon log contains "self-supervision: saver-membership lost for".
  - _portal-saver session still exists post-eject (eject does not destroy saver).
- Teardown: tmux kill-server; portaltest t.Cleanup runs.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Tagged integration build tag.
- Uses portaltest.NewIsolatedStateEnv + portalbintest.BuildPortalBinary.
- Stages daemon.pid with known-dead PID (not absent) — alternative staging from spec Test staging note, complementing 5-5.
- _portal-saver pre-created with placeholder (sh -c 'exec tail -f /dev/null') so daemon's PID cannot match pane_pid.
- Daemon exits with code 0 within (N+1) * TickerPeriod + 2s.
- Daemon log contains self-supervision INFO line.
- _portal-saver still exists after daemon exits (composes with Phase 3 destroy-unattached=off).

Tests:
- pid mismatch — daemon exits within (N+1) ticks when _portal-saver pane process is not the daemon
- pid mismatch — daemon log records self-supervision INFO line
- pid mismatch — _portal-saver session survives the eject
- pid mismatch — exit code 0, no shutdown handler invocation

Edge Cases:
- Staged dead PID must be reliably dead: cmd := exec.Command("true"); cmd.Run(); deadPID := cmd.Process.Pid (after Run returns, reaped + PID dead). Reuse pattern from Phase 4 C tests.
- tmux respawn-pane -k usable mid-test for reinforcement; can exec.Command("tmux", "-S", socket, "respawn-pane", ...) rather than portal client wrapper.
- Subtle confound: if daemon happens to be pane process at startup (misconfigured setup), self-check passes + test hangs. Pre-action verification daemon.PID != pane_pid REQUIRED.
- Test must NOT call portal bootstrap code path — only raw daemon subprocess + direct tmux invocations.

Spec Reference: § Component D acceptance: Self-eject on saver pane pid mismatch. § Test staging note suggests tmux respawn-pane -k -t _portal-saver 'sh -c "exec tail -f /dev/null"'. This task variant: pre-create saver with placeholder, spawn daemon as non-saver — structurally equivalent (PID differs from pane_pid), simpler.

title: Integration test bytes-identical scrollback dir snapshot across self-eject
description:
Component D correctness hinges on divergent-view daemon NOT executing one more captureAndCommit / gcOrphanScrollback on its way out — destructive operation os.Exit(0) eject exists to prevent. Code-review-only enforcement insufficient. Spec mandates empirical assertion scrollback dir bytes-identical at first failing tick (before counter reaches N) and immediately after eject. See phase-5-tasks.md task 5-7 for full detail.

Solution: Integration test spawns real portal state daemon subprocess under conditions guaranteed to trigger self-eject (absent-saver from 5-5), snapshots scrollback dir at moment first failing tick fires (before counter reaches N), waits for daemon exit, snapshots again post-exit, asserts byte-identical: no new .bin, no removed .bin, no size/mtime/content delta.

Do:
- Place alongside 5-5/5-6 in cmd/state_daemon_self_supervision_integration_test.go.
- Setup mirrors 5-5 (absent-saver trigger):
  1. env, stateDir := portaltest.NewIsolatedStateEnv(t).
  2. binPath := portalbintest.BuildPortalBinary(t).
  3. Real tmux via tmuxtest custom socket; do NOT create _portal-saver.
  4. Stage <stateDir>/daemon.pid as absent.
- Snapshot helper:
  type fileSnap struct{ size int64; mtimeNS int64; sum [32]byte }
  func snapshotDir(t *testing.T, dir string) map[string]fileSnap { ... }
  For each regular file under <stateDir>/scrollback/ (recursive, lstat semantics): size, mtime ns, SHA-256. Use filepath.WalkDir. If dir doesn't exist (no scrollback written) → empty map (valid).
- Diff helper: compare two snapshots; return list of deltas (added, removed, size, mtime, content). Test fails on any non-empty delta.
- Action:
  1. Spawn daemon subprocess.
  2. Wait for first probe-false tick: poll daemon log for first WARN/INFO non-self-supervision emission, OR fixed delay 1*TickerPeriod + 200ms.
  3. snapBefore := snapshotDir(t, scrollbackDir).
  4. Wait for daemon to exit (poll FindProcess + Signal(0) ESRCH, bounded (N+1)*TickerPeriod + 2s).
  5. snapAfter := snapshotDir(t, scrollbackDir).
  6. Assert diff(snapBefore, snapAfter) empty.
- Snapshot intentionally during failing-tick window (counter > 0 but < N) to catch single per-tick capture/commit slip.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Tagged integration build tag.
- Uses portaltest.NewIsolatedStateEnv + portalbintest.BuildPortalBinary.
- snapshotDir records size + mtime ns + SHA-256 for every regular file under <stateDir>/scrollback/ (recursive); lstat semantics.
- snapBefore captured at or after first failing-tick observation, before counter reaches N.
- snapAfter captured immediately after exit (verified via Signal(0) ESRCH).
- Diff returns empty delta list.
- Asserts exit code 0.
- Empty pre-snapshot (no scrollback ever written) is valid baseline.

Tests:
- scrollback directory bytes-identical at first-failing-tick and post-exit (absent-saver trigger)
- empty pre-snapshot remains empty post-exit (no spurious writes on eject path)

Edge Cases:
- Window between first failing tick and exit short ((N-1)*TickerPeriod max). Polling cadence 50ms on log or fixed-delay variant.
- mtime granularity on macOS HFS+/APFS sub-second; SHA-256 + size is load-bearing. mtime supplementary — log for debug but accept some flakiness.
- <stateDir>/scrollback/ may not exist — snapshotDir returns empty; diff against empty also empty → pass legitimately.
- First failing tick may produce no scrollback file (daemon has no _portal-saver + no live sessions) — pre-snapshot may be empty. Still valid: assertion is no change, not non-empty initial state.

Spec Reference: § Component D acceptance: No final flush on self-eject. Snapshot at first failing tick + post-os.Exit(0); must be identical (no new files, no deletions, no mtime/size changes). Mirrors Phase 4 Task 4-2 (escalation-killed orphans) — same pattern applied to os.Exit(0) path.

title: Integration test legitimate first-tick self-check inside fresh _portal-saver
description:
Self-check must NOT false-positive on legitimate daemon's first tick. Legitimate daemon is saver pane process of freshly-created _portal-saver — os.Getpid() matches pane pid by construction. If self-check wired incorrectly (off-by-one in counter, probe miscompares pids as strings vs ints, HasSession race during early bootstrap), legitimate daemon would self-eject on tick 1, breaking entire Portal experience. False-positive cold-start regression guard. See phase-5-tasks.md task 5-8 for full detail.

Solution: Integration test composes with Phase 3 placeholder-then-respawn ordering — bootstrap fresh _portal-saver via production path, wait for respawned daemon to become healthy, observe daemon ticking normally for ≥(N+2) * TickerPeriod without exiting. Asserts daemon still alive, counter (inferred from absence of self-supervision INFO in logs) stayed at 0, daemon.pid reflects live daemon PID matching _portal-saver pane pid.

Do:
- Place alongside 5-5/5-6/5-7 in cmd/state_daemon_self_supervision_integration_test.go.
- Setup:
  1. env, stateDir := portaltest.NewIsolatedStateEnv(t).
  2. binPath := portalbintest.BuildPortalBinary(t).
  3. Real tmux server via tmuxtest custom socket.
  4. Invoke BootstrapPortalSaver (via test entry point, or short-lived helper portal open --no-tui — verify cleanest seam during scaffolding). Goal: exercise PRODUCTION saver-creation path so Phase 3's placeholder-then-respawn ordering is on test path.
- Action: wait for daemon to acquire lock + first ticks (≥(N+2) * TickerPeriod).
- Assertion:
  - Daemon process still alive after (N+2) * TickerPeriod.
  - Daemon log does NOT contain "self-supervision: saver-membership lost".
  - daemon.pid contents = _portal-saver pane PID (via tmux list-panes -F '#{pane_pid}').
  - Counter stayed at 0 (inferred from log absence).
- Teardown: gracefully shut down daemon + tmux; portaltest backstop runs.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Tagged integration build tag.
- Uses portaltest.NewIsolatedStateEnv + portalbintest.BuildPortalBinary.
- Exercises Phase 3 placeholder-then-respawn cold-start path.
- Daemon alive after (N+2) * TickerPeriod.
- Log free of self-supervision INFO line.
- daemon.pid = _portal-saver pane PID.
- Composes with Phase 3 ordering — if Phase 3 introduces transient window where pane pid disagrees at tick 1, the single-digit-ticks hysteresis (N≥3) absorbs the transient + test still passes.

Tests:
- legitimate daemon ticking inside fresh _portal-saver does not self-eject within (N+2) ticks
- legitimate daemon log free of self-supervision INFO during cold-start window
- daemon.pid matches _portal-saver pane PID throughout cold-start

Edge Cases:
- Phase 3 cold-start may have transient mismatch in first tick before respawned daemon settles — hysteresis (N≥3) absorbs.
- BootstrapPortalSaver invocation path: verify cleanest test seam (test entry point or short-lived helper subprocess).
- Daemon may take 1-2s to acquire lock + complete first tick — observation window starts AFTER acquire confirmed.

Spec Reference: § Component D acceptance: Legitimate first-tick self-check passes inside freshly-created _portal-saver (pane pid matches os.Getpid()). Composes with Phase 3 saver creation ordering.

title: Unit test selfSupervisionHysteresisTicks >= 1
description:
Hysteresis constant must never accidentally be zero. Spec calls out this guard explicitly: "A unit test asserts selfSupervisionHysteresisTicks >= 1 to prevent accidental zeroing." Trivial test but distinct target (compile-time constant) from probe-stubbed loop tests. See phase-5-tasks.md task 5-9 for full detail.

Solution: Single trivial unit test asserting selfSupervisionHysteresisTicks >= 1. Deliberately weak per spec — guards against accidental zeroing without overconstraining future tuning.

Do:
- Place in cmd/state_daemon_self_supervision_test.go (or sibling). No integration build tag.
- Test:
  func TestSelfSupervisionHysteresisTicks_LowerBound(t *testing.T) {
      if selfSupervisionHysteresisTicks < 1 {
          t.Fatalf("selfSupervisionHysteresisTicks must be >= 1, got %d", selfSupervisionHysteresisTicks)
      }
  }
- No t.Parallel() per CLAUDE.md.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Test exists in cmd/state_daemon_self_supervision_test.go.
- Asserts selfSupervisionHysteresisTicks >= 1.
- No t.Parallel.
- Passes against the production value from Task 5-1 (which will be ≥3).
- Fails loudly if someone sets N=0.

Tests:
- selfSupervisionHysteresisTicks lower bound is at least 1

Edge Cases:
- Compile-time constant accessibility — test must be in same package as cmd (cmd/...).
- No t.Parallel contention.
- Deliberately weak — does NOT assert upper bound (Task 5-1's clamp handles that).

Spec Reference: § Component D — "A unit test asserts selfSupervisionHysteresisTicks >= 1 to prevent accidental zeroing."

title: Build composite test harness with three-daemon scenario setup
description:
Composite harness reconstructing reporter's failure scenario for end-to-end ship-readiness verification. Builds on Phase 4 task 4-10's A+B+C harness; lifts shared scaffolding; adds D/E/F-relevant setup. See phase-6-tasks.md task 6-1 for full detail.

Solution: Real-tmux composite harness in cmd/bootstrap/composition_e2e_integration_test.go (or internal/restoretest/). Spawns 1 saver-pane daemon + 2 orphan daemons (one with daemon.pid reference, one without). Includes user sessions with seeded pane output so scrollback dir observation is meaningful. All under integration build tag.

Do:
- Place test under existing integration build tag, file cmd/bootstrap/composition_e2e_integration_test.go (or internal/restoretest/ if real-tmux scaffolding cleaner — choose per existing precedent).
- Setup helper compositeHarness(t *testing.T) returns (env, stateDir, sock, daemonPIDs []int):
  1. env, stateDir := portaltest.NewIsolatedStateEnv(t) — Phase 1 helper.
  2. binPath := portalbintest.BuildPortalBinary(t).
  3. sock := tmuxtest.New(t) — custom socket.
  4. Create 2 user sessions on tmux server with non-trivial seeded pane output (e.g., echo loop or seeded scrollback) so subsequent scrollback observation is meaningful.
  5. Create _portal-saver via production path: placeholder → set destroy-unattached=off → respawn-pane -k to portal state daemon (composes with Phase 3 ordering). Wait until legitimate daemon writes daemon.pid.
  6. Spawn 2 orphan daemons as direct subprocesses (NOT via tmux). Both have isolated env, same stateDir. Parent process is test, NOT saver pane — they ARE orphans.
  7. Orphan 1: starts after legitimate, overwrites daemon.pid with own PID (simulating reporter's orphan-with-daemon.pid case).
  8. Orphan 2: starts but does NOT write daemon.pid — only visible via pgrep.
- Teardown via t.Cleanup (LIFO order): kill all spawned daemons (SIGKILL with reap), kill tmux server, portaltest backstop runs.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Tagged with existing integration build tag.
- Uses portaltest.NewIsolatedStateEnv + portalbintest.BuildPortalBinary + tmuxtest.
- 3 daemons spawned: 1 legitimate (saver-pane), 2 orphans (1 with daemon.pid reference, 1 without).
- daemon.pid references orphan 1's PID (not legitimate's, not orphan 2's).
- Both orphan parent processes differ from saver pane process (verified via ps).
- 2 user sessions exist on tmux server with seeded pane output (scrollback observability).
- Teardown kills all spawned daemons even on assertion failure (kill -9 + wait for reap in t.Cleanup).
- portaltest backstop registered for end-of-test fingerprint check.

Tests:
- harness builds three-daemon scenario reproducing reporter's failure conditions
- harness teardown reaps all spawned daemons on success
- harness teardown reaps all spawned daemons on assertion failure (deferred reap via t.Cleanup)

Edge Cases:
- Integration build tag pattern matches existing precedent (verify via grep before adding new tag).
- portaltest.NewIsolatedStateEnv reused (do not re-implement env isolation).
- daemon.pid references one orphan only — explicit assertion in setup to prevent silent misconfiguration.
- Both orphan parents differ from saver pane process — verified via os.Getppid() comparison.
- tmuxtest scaffolding reused — do not roll new tmux fixture.
- Teardown via t.Cleanup ensures even panics + assertion failures still reap daemons (no leaked subprocesses).

Spec Reference: § Composite End-to-End Verification: single composite integration test reconstructing reporter's failure scenario. Three concurrent daemons (1 legitimate saver-pane + 2 orphans, one with daemon.pid reference + one without).

title: Assert pre-fix three-daemon state reproduces under harness
description:
Reproduce reporter's failure conditions under composite harness BEFORE invoking bootstrap. pgrep -fxc == 3 and scrollback dir oscillates 0-1 .bin across multiple ticks. See phase-6-tasks.md task 6-2 for full detail.

Solution: After harness setup (6-1), assert pgrep -fxc 'portal state daemon' returns 3 via canonical form (matches spawn pattern). Observe scrollback dir oscillation across 4 samples over ~3.6s (avoids 0-1 boundary flake on single sample). Establish baseline of pre-fix dysfunction before bootstrap convergence (6-3).

Do:
- After compositeHarness setup, before invoking bootstrap, run pre-fix assertions:
  1. pgrep -fxc 'portal state daemon' returns 3.
  2. Observe scrollback dir oscillation: take 4 samples of <stateDir>/scrollback/ across 3.6s (sample every 900ms). Assert .bin count varies (not all same value) across samples — proves the GC race is active.
  3. Multiple samples avoid 0-1 boundary flake: if a single sample happens to hit a quiet moment, the variance check still passes.

Acceptance Criteria:
- pgrep -fxc 'portal state daemon' canonical form (matches Phase 4 Component B's enumeration) returns exactly 3.
- 4 scrollback dir samples taken over ~3.6s; at least 2 distinct .bin counts observed across samples (proves oscillation).
- Variance check avoids 0-1 boundary flake on single sample.
- No false-positive convergence pre-bootstrap (pgrep != 1).
- Pre-fix dysfunction established as baseline; subsequent 6-3 convergence is meaningful.

Tests:
- pre-fix: pgrep -fxc 'portal state daemon' returns 3
- pre-fix: scrollback dir .bin count varies across 4 samples (oscillation observed)
- pre-fix: pgrep != 1 (no false-positive convergence)

Edge Cases:
- pgrep canonical form must match harness spawn (e.g., '^portal state daemon( |$)' or 'portal state daemon').
- Scrollback oscillation observed across multiple ticks not single sample — daemon TickerPeriod is 1s.
- Harness avoids 0-1 boundary flake via variance check.
- No false-positive convergence — pgrep != 1 confirms scenario is correctly set up.

Spec Reference: § Composite End-to-End Verification: confirm pre-fix state reproduces (pgrep -fxc == 3, scrollback dir oscillates 0-1 .bin across ticks).

title: Invoke bootstrap and assert pgrep convergence to 1 within 6 s
description:
A+B composition convergence assertion via test entry point. Within 6s of EnsureSaver entry, pgrep -fxc must return 1 and surviving daemon must be the saver-pane process. See phase-6-tasks.md task 6-3 for full detail.

Solution: Invoke production bootstrap orchestrator (Orchestrator.Run, preferred) or portal open subprocess. Start timer at EnsureSaver entry (preferred — direct wrap) or Orchestrator.Run entry (fallback, conservative). Poll pgrep until 1 within 6s. Survivor PID must equal _portal-saver pane PID. Drain bootstrap warnings + assert absence of "no such session" and "prior daemon did not exit" entries.

Do:
- Instrument timer start. Preferred: direct Orchestrator.Run wrap measuring EnsureSaver entry; fallback: Orchestrator.Run entry time (slightly conservative — adds startup latency to budget). Document choice in test.
- Invoke production Orchestrator.Run directly (preferred over portal open subprocess — cleaner test surface). Construct orchestrator via internal/bootstrapadapter.NewProductionOrchestrator.
- Poll pgrep -fxc 'portal state daemon' until returns 1, bounded 6s from EnsureSaver entry. Fail with current count if no convergence.
- Identify survivor: tmux list-panes -t _portal-saver -F '#{pane_pid}' returns saver pane PID. Assert survivor PID = saver pane PID (not just any one of the three).
- Drain bootstrap warnings via Orchestrator.Run return value (warnings slice). Assert:
  - Zero "no such session: _portal-saver" entries.
  - Zero "prior daemon did not exit" or similar Component-A timeout entries.
- Use portalbintest binary (built fresh) so version-checked code paths run.

Acceptance Criteria:
- 6s budget measured from EnsureSaver entry (preferred) or Orchestrator.Run entry (documented fallback).
- pgrep -fxc 'portal state daemon' returns 1 within budget.
- Survivor PID = _portal-saver pane PID via tmux list-panes -F '#{pane_pid}'.
- Bootstrap warnings drained from Orchestrator.Run return.
- Zero "no such session: _portal-saver" warnings.
- Zero "prior daemon did not exit" warnings.
- Uses portalbintest-built binary.

Tests:
- composition: pgrep -fxc converges to 1 within 6s of EnsureSaver entry
- composition: survivor PID equals _portal-saver pane PID (not orphan)
- composition: bootstrap warnings contain zero 'no such session: _portal-saver' entries
- composition: bootstrap warnings contain zero 'prior daemon did not exit' entries

Edge Cases:
- 6s budget tight — measure from EnsureSaver entry not test start. Direct Orchestrator wrap preferred; fallback is conservative.
- Surviving daemon is saver-pane process not just any one — explicit PID match assertion.
- portalbintest binary used so any version-checked logic runs against the built binary.
- Bootstrap warnings drained — Orchestrator.Run return value.

Spec Reference: § Composite End-to-End Verification: post-bootstrap pgrep -fxc == 1 within 6s of EnsureSaver entry. 6s = A escalation budget (5s + 1s) + B sub-second sweep.

title: Assert scrollback directory stability across 10x1 s observations post-bootstrap
description:
A+B+E composition assertion: once singleton enforced, no .bin deletions or unexpected new files across 10 consecutive 1s observations post-bootstrap. Legitimate per-tick .bin writes for live panes ARE allowed (mtime updates); deletions or unexpected new files fail. See phase-6-tasks.md task 6-4 for full detail.

Solution: After 6-3 convergence, observe scrollback dir stability. Polled os.ReadDir snapshots (no fsnotify needed). Every 1s for 10 observations, snapshot <stateDir>/scrollback/ as set of paths. Assert path-set is invariant across observations (no path appears/disappears mid-window). mtime updates on existing .bin files are LEGITIMATE (live capture); only deletions or new file appearances signal regression.

Do:
- After 6-3 convergence assertion, wait one TickerPeriod (1s) to let observation window start after first post-bootstrap tick.
- Every 1s for 10 observations: snapshot <stateDir>/scrollback/ as set of relative paths (filepath.WalkDir, regular files only).
- Compare each snapshot to baseline (first snapshot). Assert path-set invariant — no path added or removed across the 10-sample window.
- mtime + content updates on existing .bin files are LEGITIMATE (live pane capture). Distinguish update from oscillation: only path-set membership changes fail. Size + content + mtime are NOT compared in this stability test (they would falsely flag legitimate per-tick captures).

Acceptance Criteria:
- Observation window starts AFTER first post-bootstrap tick (waits 1s post-convergence).
- 10 samples of <stateDir>/scrollback/ across 10s (sample every 1s).
- Path-set invariant across all 10 samples (no path added or removed).
- mtime updates on existing .bin files are allowed (legitimate per-tick capture for live panes).
- Test distinguishes legitimate update from oscillation: only path-set membership changes fail.
- Polled os.ReadDir snapshots (no fsnotify required per spec).

Tests:
- post-bootstrap: scrollback dir path-set invariant across 10x1s observations
- post-bootstrap: legitimate per-tick .bin writes (mtime updates) do not fail stability assertion
- post-bootstrap: any .bin deletion or unexpected new file fails the test

Edge Cases:
- Legitimate per-tick .bin writes for live panes ARE allowed — mtime + content update tolerated; only deletions/new files fail.
- Diff distinguishes update from oscillation — path-set membership is the load-bearing invariant.
- Observation window starts AFTER first post-bootstrap tick (1s buffer).
- Polled os.ReadDir acceptable; fsnotify not required.

Spec Reference: § Composite End-to-End Verification: scrollback dir stable across 10 consecutive 1s observations — no .bin deletions, no unexpected new files (A+B+E composition).

title: Assert fresh-process AcquireDaemonLock refuses with ErrDaemonLockHeld post-bootstrap
description:
Component C pre-check on live state. After 6-3 convergence, a fresh-process AcquireDaemonLock invocation (separate process, not test goroutine) must refuse with ErrDaemonLockHeld — verifying the pre-check path is exercised (not the EWOULDBLOCK fallback). See phase-6-tasks.md task 6-5 for full detail.

Solution: From separate subprocess (or in-test goroutine that re-runs full acquire flow), invoke state.AcquireDaemonLock(stateDir). Must return state.ErrDaemonLockHeld. Subprocess invocation preferred — exercises Component C pre-check path with fresh stat() / open() syscalls + verifies sentinel ExitCode flows out cleanly.

Do:
- After 6-3 convergence + 6-4 stability assertions, invoke AcquireDaemonLock from separate process:
  - Preferred: small helper subprocess (subcommand of portal binary built via portalbintest, OR fork-with-flag pattern using TestMain). Subprocess attempts state.AcquireDaemonLock(stateDir) and exits with sentinel code 42 on ErrDaemonLockHeld, 1 on success (unexpected), 2 on any other error.
  - cmd := exec.Command(binPath, ...sentinel command...); cmd.Env = env (isolated state dir).
  - Wait for subprocess exit; assert exit code == 42.
- Alternative (in-test goroutine): run state.AcquireDaemonLock directly. Less robust but simpler. Document choice in test.
- Verify pre-check path (not EWOULDBLOCK): assert error is errors.Is(err, state.ErrDaemonLockHeld) AND check via instrumentation that lockAcquire (the flock call) was NOT called. Phase 4 Component C pre-check returns ErrDaemonLockHeld BEFORE opening daemon.lock.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Fresh-process AcquireDaemonLock invocation returns state.ErrDaemonLockHeld.
- Preferred: separate subprocess; subprocess exit code == 42 (sentinel for ErrDaemonLockHeld).
- legitimate daemon.pid is fresh + identity-checks (Phase 4 C pre-check sees IdentifyIsPortalDaemon).
- Pre-check path exercised — daemon.lock NOT opened during refusal (verified via instrumentation or stubbed seam if practical; otherwise trust source via Task 4-6's acceptance).
- No destructive coexistence on refusal — survivor daemon untouched, sessions.json unchanged.

Tests:
- post-bootstrap: fresh-process state.AcquireDaemonLock returns ErrDaemonLockHeld
- post-bootstrap: refusal via Component C pre-check (not EWOULDBLOCK fallback)
- post-bootstrap: refused subprocess does not destructively coexist (sessions.json untouched)

Edge Cases:
- Invocation from separate process (preferred) — subprocess with sentinel exit code 42.
- Legitimate daemon.pid is fresh — pre-check sees IdentifyIsPortalDaemon, returns ErrDaemonLockHeld immediately.
- Pre-check path vs EWOULDBLOCK fallback: pre-check refuses BEFORE opening daemon.lock; instrument if practical to verify.
- No destructive coexistence: refused acquirer exits cleanly without writing.

Spec Reference: § Composite End-to-End Verification: fresh-process AcquireDaemonLock invocation refuses with ErrDaemonLockHeld (Component C pre-check verified on live state).

title: Assert Component D self-eject in live context after external saver-pane kill
description:
Component D in live context. After convergence + stability assertions, externally kill the saver pane; assert daemon self-ejects within (N+1) * TickerPeriod. Verifies D composes with A+B+C+E+F in real bootstrap state. See phase-6-tasks.md task 6-6 for full detail.

Solution: Use tmux respawn-pane to externally replace _portal-saver pane process (Phase 5 Task 5-6 has canonical form — consult before using). Daemon's saverMembershipProbe will detect mismatch within hysteresis window. Assert daemon process exits within (selfSupervisionHysteresisTicks+1)*TickerPeriod. Scrollback bytes-identical across eject window (composes with D's no-final-flush from Phase 5 Task 5-7).

Do:
- After 6-3 convergence + 6-4 stability + 6-5 lock refusal + 6-7 F observables assertions complete, run the destructive D sub-scenario LAST (since it kills the saver-pane daemon).
- Pre-eject snapshot: snapBefore := snapshotDir(t, scrollbackDir) — using fingerprint helper from Phase 4 Task 4-2.
- Externally replace saver pane: consult Phase 5 Task 5-6 for canonical respawn-pane form. NOTE: -k flag would SIGKILL the daemon directly (bypassing self-check) — use whichever Phase 5 settled on. Likely tmux respawn-pane -t _portal-saver 'sh -c "exec tail -f /dev/null"' (no -k flag), forcing pane process replacement without immediate SIGKILL, so daemon detects via self-check rather than dying first.
- Wait for daemon process to exit. Bound: (selfSupervisionHysteresisTicks + 1) * TickerPeriod + 2s slack. Verify via Signal(0) ESRCH on legitimate daemon PID.
- Post-eject snapshot: snapAfter := snapshotDir(t, scrollbackDir).
- Assert exit code 0 (os.Exit(0) — no defaultShutdownFlush).
- Assert diff(snapBefore, snapAfter) byte-identical (no new .bin, no deletions, no size/mtime/content changes).
- Assert daemon log contains "self-supervision: saver-membership lost for" INFO entry.
- Stale daemon.pid left in place (Phase 5 Task 5-3 contract). Assert daemon.pid still exists post-eject with the dead PID.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Sub-scenario runs LAST among Phase 6 assertions (destroys saver-pane daemon).
- External kill via tmux respawn-pane (canonical form per Phase 5 Task 5-6).
- Daemon process exits within (selfSupervisionHysteresisTicks + 1) * TickerPeriod + 2s slack.
- Exit code 0 (os.Exit(0) path).
- Scrollback dir bytes-identical pre- and post-eject (composes with Phase 5 Task 5-7).
- Daemon log contains self-supervision INFO entry.
- Stale daemon.pid left in place.
- N+1 derived from selfSupervisionHysteresisTicks constant (Phase 5 Task 5-1).

Tests:
- live context: daemon self-ejects within (N+1) ticks after external saver pane process replacement
- live context: scrollback dir bytes-identical across self-eject window (no final flush)
- live context: daemon exits with code 0 (os.Exit(0), not graceful shutdown)
- live context: daemon log records self-supervision INFO line
- live context: stale daemon.pid left in place

Edge Cases:
- External kill form: -k flag would SIGKILL bypassing self-check — use form that replaces pane process without immediate kill (consult Phase 5 Task 5-6 for exact form).
- Daemon process wait within (N+1) tick intervals — bound derived from selfSupervisionHysteresisTicks not hardcoded.
- Scrollback bytes-identical across eject — composes with D's no-final-flush guarantee.
- Sub-scenario LAST (destroys saver-pane daemon) — other assertions must complete first.
- Phase 5 Task 5-6 has canonical respawn-pane form — defer to it for invocation pattern.

Spec Reference: § Composite End-to-End Verification: externally killing legitimate daemon's _portal-saver pane triggers self-eject within (N+1) tick intervals (Component D in live context).

title: Assert Component F end-state observables on _portal-saver
description:
Component F end-state observables. Post-bootstrap: _portal-saver's pane process is portal state daemon AND tmux show-options -t _portal-saver destroy-unattached reports off. Runs after readiness barrier (no race) and BEFORE Component-D eject sub-scenario (6-6, which destroys the pane process). See phase-6-tasks.md task 6-7 for full detail.

Solution: After 6-3 convergence + 6-4 stability assertions, before 6-6 destructive eject, query tmux observables:
- tmux list-panes -t _portal-saver -F '#{pane_pid}' returns pane PID.
- ps -o args= -p <pane_pid> returns args containing "portal state daemon".
- tmux show-options -t _portal-saver destroy-unattached output (trimmed/quoted correctly) contains "off".

Do:
- Place between 6-5 (lock refusal) and 6-6 (D eject) in composite test sequence.
- Query tmux observables:
  1. Pane PID: tmux -S <socket> list-panes -t _portal-saver -F '#{pane_pid}'. Parse single integer pid.
  2. Pane process: ps -o args= -p <pid>. Trim whitespace. Assert contains "portal state daemon" (not "tail -f /dev/null" placeholder, not "sh -c" wrapper).
  3. destroy-unattached: tmux -S <socket> show-options -t _portal-saver destroy-unattached. Output format: "destroy-unattached off" (verify quoting + whitespace). Assert value == "off".
- All queries run after Phase 3's readiness barrier (Task 3-3) has settled (6-3 convergence implies barrier complete).
- Run BEFORE 6-6's destructive eject — 6-6 destroys pane process, would invalidate F observables.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Runs after 6-3 convergence (readiness barrier complete) and BEFORE 6-6 destructive eject.
- tmux list-panes -F '#{pane_pid}' returns single integer; ps -o args= -p <pid> contains "portal state daemon".
- tmux show-options -t _portal-saver destroy-unattached output parses to value == "off".
- show-options output trimmed + quoted correctly (handles tmux's quoting).
- No race against readiness barrier — query after 6-3 convergence.

Tests:
- post-bootstrap: _portal-saver pane process is portal state daemon (via list-panes + ps -o args=)
- post-bootstrap: tmux show-options -t _portal-saver destroy-unattached == off
- post-bootstrap: F observables stable across multiple queries (no transient state)

Edge Cases:
- Pane process verified via list-panes -F '#{pane_pid}' + ps -o args= on that pid (not just placeholder existence).
- show-options output trimmed + quoted: tmux may emit "destroy-unattached off" or with quotes — parse robustly via tmuxout.StripMatchedOuterQuotes if needed.
- Runs after readiness barrier so no race against initial respawn settling.
- Runs BEFORE Component-D eject (6-6) — 6-6 destroys pane.

Spec Reference: § Composite End-to-End Verification: post-bootstrap _portal-saver's pane process is portal state daemon AND tmux show-options -t _portal-saver destroy-unattached reports off (Component F).

title: Assert portaltest cleanup fingerprint backstop reports clean on test exit
description:
End-state verification: portaltest cleanup fingerprint diff passes (developer state untouched throughout test). Final ship-readiness gate confirming the composite test isolates correctly. See phase-6-tasks.md task 6-8 for full detail.

Solution: Phase 1 Task 1-3's t.Cleanup fingerprint-diff backstop runs automatically on test exit. Test must NOT manually invoke it — backstop fires via t.Cleanup (LIFO order ensures harness teardown precedes fingerprint walk). Also add audit grep of test file to ensure no unisolated state.AcquireDaemonLock or exec.Command calls leaked past env override.

Do:
- The t.Cleanup backstop from portaltest.NewIsolatedStateEnv (Phase 1 Task 1-3) fires automatically — no explicit invocation required.
- Verify LIFO ordering: harness teardown (kill daemons, kill tmux) runs BEFORE fingerprint walk. Cleanup order:
  1. (Registered last → fires first): kill spawned daemons via SIGKILL + wait for reap.
  2. (Registered before daemons): kill tmux server via tmux kill-server.
  3. (Registered first via portaltest.NewIsolatedStateEnv → fires last): fingerprint-diff walk of ~/.config/portal/state/.
- Per Go testing.T.Cleanup semantics: cleanups run in reverse registration order (LIFO). portaltest.NewIsolatedStateEnv registers fingerprint walk on FIRST call (before any other Cleanup registered by harness), so it fires LAST — after all harness teardown.
- Optional positive control: log isolated state dir contents on success (helps debug future state-leak regressions).
- Optional audit: grep test source file for unisolated state.AcquireDaemonLock or exec.Command calls (without env override). If found, fail compile via go:build assertion or add a separate audit test.

Acceptance Criteria:
- portaltest fingerprint-diff backstop runs via t.Cleanup automatically (no explicit invocation in Phase 6 test code).
- Cleanup LIFO order: harness teardown precedes fingerprint walk.
- Harness teardown (kill daemons + tmux server) completes BEFORE backstop walks ~/.config/portal/state/.
- Backstop reports clean — no fingerprint deltas on developer state dir.
- No late-write race after assertions complete.
- Optional audit: test file grep for unisolated AcquireDaemonLock / exec.Command (if implemented).

Tests:
- end-state: portaltest fingerprint diff reports clean (no mutations to ~/.config/portal/state/)
- end-state: cleanup LIFO order ensures harness teardown precedes fingerprint walk
- end-state: no late-write race after composite assertions complete

Edge Cases:
- Backstop runs via t.Cleanup so failures still report even on assertion panic.
- No late-write race: assertions complete + cleanups run synchronously.
- Harness teardown completes before fingerprint walk: LIFO registration order critical.
- portaltest.NewIsolatedStateEnv registered FIRST (in harness setup) so it fires LAST in LIFO — ideal.

Spec Reference: § Composite End-to-End Verification: test uses portaltest.NewIsolatedStateEnv for state-dir isolation; no developer-state mutations on test exit.

title: Phase 7: Analysis (Cycle 1)
Consolidate fingerprint diff/format/sort helpers into internal/portaltest
description:
**Problem**: Three independent integration-test files re-implement the same five-helper diff suite over `portaltest.Fingerprint` maps (~400 LOC combined), and a fourth near-twin lives as `emitFieldDeltas` in `internal/portaltest/fingerprint.go:203-226`. Any change to `Fingerprint`'s shape requires four coordinated edits with no compile-time guard.

**Solution**: Promote a single consolidated helper into `internal/portaltest`. New API: `DiffFingerprints(pre, post map[string]Fingerprint) []FingerprintDelta` returning structured deltas, plus `FormatFingerprint(fp) string` and `FormatDelta(d FingerprintDelta) string`. Refactor the three test files and the package-internal `emitFieldDeltas`/`reportStateDirDelta` to consume the new helpers.

**Outcome**: Single source of truth for fingerprint diffing/formatting. Shape changes to `Fingerprint` become single-site edits with compile-time fan-out. ~300 LOC removed across test files.

**Do**:
1. Add `FingerprintDelta` struct + `DiffFingerprints`/`FormatFingerprint`/`FormatDelta` in `internal/portaltest/fingerprint.go`.
2. Refactor `internal/portaltest`'s own `emitFieldDeltas` / `reportStateDirDelta` to delegate to the new helpers.
3. Delete `assertSnapshotsEqual` + four siblings from `internal/tmux/kill_barrier_escalation_no_final_flush_integration_test.go:359-475`; replace call sites with `t.Fatalf` wrapping `DiffFingerprints` + `FormatDelta`.
4. Repeat for `cmd/state_daemon_self_supervision_integration_test.go:888-1026`.
5. Repeat for `cmd/bootstrap/composition_abc_integration_test.go:300-446`.
6. Run `go test ./...` to confirm parity.

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- `DiffFingerprints`, `FormatFingerprint`, `FormatDelta` exist as exported symbols in `internal/portaltest`.
- Zero local re-implementations of fingerprint diff/format/sort logic remain across the three integration-test files.
- `internal/portaltest`'s own `emitFieldDeltas` either calls `DiffFingerprints` or is removed in favor of it.
- `go build ./...` and `go test -tags integration ./...` pass.
- Net LOC reduction across the four files is at least 250.

**Tests**:
- Unit test `DiffFingerprints` in `internal/portaltest/fingerprint_test.go` covering: identical maps, additions-only, removals-only, field-mutation, mixed.
- Unit test `FormatDelta` for stable output ordering (sorted keys).
- Existing integration tests must continue to pass unchanged in semantics.

title: Collapse spawnOrphanDaemonIsolated and spawnOrphanDaemonIsolatedNamed
description:
**Problem**: `spawnOrphanDaemonIsolated` (orphan_sweep_integration_test.go:455-467) and `spawnOrphanDaemonIsolatedNamed` (composition_e2e_harness_integration_test.go:383-395) are line-for-line identical in the same `package bootstrap_test`, differing only in return signature (`*exec.Cmd` vs `(*exec.Cmd, string)`).

**Solution**: Keep the Named variant (strict superset), drop the suffix, delete the un-Named twin, update call sites to use `_` for the second return when unused.

**Outcome**: One spawn helper in `bootstrap_test`. Net -13 LOC.

**Do**:
1. Rename `spawnOrphanDaemonIsolatedNamed` to `spawnOrphanDaemonIsolated` in `cmd/bootstrap/composition_e2e_harness_integration_test.go`.
2. Delete the original `spawnOrphanDaemonIsolated` body from `cmd/bootstrap/orphan_sweep_integration_test.go:455-467`.
3. Audit all callers; change `cmd := spawnOrphanDaemonIsolated(...)` to `cmd, _ := spawnOrphanDaemonIsolated(...)` where unused.
4. Run `go test -tags integration ./cmd/bootstrap/...`.

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- Exactly one definition of `spawnOrphanDaemonIsolated` exists in `package bootstrap_test`.
- All call sites compile and pass under `-tags integration`.
- Net LOC across the two files reduced by at least 10.

**Tests**:
- Existing `cmd/bootstrap` integration tests must continue to pass.

title: Promote applyHostNoiseMitigation into internal/portaltest
description:
**Problem**: The two-line helper `t.Setenv("HOME", t.TempDir()); t.Setenv("XDG_CONFIG_HOME", "")` is inlined in three test packages (cmd/bootstrap, cmd, internal/tmux), each with a ~12-line apologetic rationale comment, because the canonical copy in a sibling `_test` file cannot be cross-imported.

**Solution**: Promote into `internal/portaltest` as `NeutralizeHostEnv(t *testing.T)` (or fold into `NewIsolatedStateEnv` so callers cannot forget the ordering invariant — preferred). Delete all three private copies.

**Outcome**: Single canonical host-noise mitigation primitive. Rationale comment lives in one place. ~36 lines of duplicated comment + body removed.

**Do**:
1. Add `NeutralizeHostEnv(t *testing.T)` to `internal/portaltest` with the consolidated rationale comment.
2. Decide whether to fold into `NewIsolatedStateEnv` (preferred) or expose separately; if folded, document the ordering invariant in `NewIsolatedStateEnv`'s godoc.
3. Delete `applyHostNoiseMitigation` from `cmd/bootstrap/orphan_sweep_integration_test.go:412-427`.
4. Delete the local twin from `cmd/state_daemon_self_supervision_integration_test.go`.
5. Delete the local twin from `internal/tmux/portal_saver_endstate_integration_test.go`.
6. Replace call sites accordingly.

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- `internal/portaltest` exports the host-env neutralization primitive (or it is folded into `NewIsolatedStateEnv` and documented).
- Zero inlined copies of the two-line body remain outside `internal/portaltest`.
- Rationale comment exists exactly once.
- `go test -tags integration ./...` passes.

**Tests**:
- All three integration tests previously calling the inlined helper continue to pass.
- If exposed as a separate function, add a basic unit test confirming env vars are cleared/reset under `t.Cleanup`.

title: Collapse duplicated identify/read-PID seam pairs in portal_saver.go
description:
**Problem**: `internal/tmux/portal_saver.go:178-279` exposes two pairs that are the same primitive under different names: (a) `killBarrierIdentifyDaemon` and `saverReadinessIdentify` both wrap `state.IdentifyDaemon`; (b) `killBarrierReadPID` and `saverReadinessReadPID` both wrap `state.ReadPIDFile`. No test composes both paths through a single stub. Package surface (12 package-level vars) is over-segmented.

**Solution**: Collapse each duplicated pair to a single seam (`saverIdentifyDaemon`, `saverReadPID`) shared between kill-barrier escalation and readiness barrier.

**Outcome**: 12 → 10 package-level seams in `internal/tmux`. Cleaner surface; one seam per primitive.

**Do**:
1. Introduce `saverIdentifyDaemon = state.IdentifyDaemon` and `saverReadPID = state.ReadPIDFile`.
2. Replace all internal references to `killBarrierIdentifyDaemon` / `saverReadinessIdentify` with `saverIdentifyDaemon`.
3. Replace all internal references to `killBarrierReadPID` / `saverReadinessReadPID` with `saverReadPID`.
4. Delete the four obsolete vars.
5. Update tests in `internal/tmux/*_test.go` that stage these seams; verify kill-barrier and readiness-barrier scenarios stage distinct outcomes through the same seam by ordering / setup-teardown.

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- Exactly two seams exist in `portal_saver.go` for identify/read-PID primitives (down from four).
- Package-level seam count reduced from 12 to 10.
- All existing kill-barrier and readiness-barrier tests pass without behavioral regression.
- `go test -tags integration ./internal/tmux/...` passes.

**Tests**:
- Existing kill-barrier escalation and readiness-barrier tests continue to pass.
- Add or adjust at least one test demonstrating the unified seam can stage distinct outcomes across the two code paths via sequential staging.

title: Colocate WriteVersionFile with WritePIDFile in defaultDaemonRun
description:
**Problem**: T4-8 moved `acquireDaemonLock` + `WritePIDFile` into `defaultDaemonRun` (cmd/state_daemon.go:189-209), but `WriteVersionFile` was left at RunE (cmd/state_daemon.go:446). Startup writes split across two functions.

**Solution**: Move `WriteVersionFile` into `defaultDaemonRun` immediately after `WritePIDFile`. The AST adjacency invariant asserts the acquire→pidfile relationship only, so colocation does not break it.

**Outcome**: All startup writes live in one function.

**Do**:
1. Move the `WriteVersionFile` call from `RunE` (cmd/state_daemon.go:446) into `defaultDaemonRun` immediately after `WritePIDFile`.
2. Run `TestDaemonAcquireLockOrdering_WritePIDFollowsAcquire` to confirm the AST invariant still holds.
3. Verify no change in error propagation / cleanup semantics.
4. Add a comment above the block listing the sequence: lock → pidfile → versionfile.

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- `WriteVersionFile` is invoked from `defaultDaemonRun`, not `RunE`.
- `TestDaemonAcquireLockOrdering_WritePIDFollowsAcquire` passes unchanged.
- All `cmd` daemon tests pass.
- `WriteVersionFile` error handling parity verified.

**Tests**:
- Existing daemon-startup tests continue to pass.
- Add a regression test asserting `WriteVersionFile` is called during `defaultDaemonRun` (check the version file exists after a successful run in an isolated state dir).

title: Document the bootstrap.Logger four-method contract
description:
**Problem**: `bootstrap.Logger` (cmd/bootstrap/bootstrap.go:171-195) gained `Info` in T4-3, bringing mandatory surface to Debug/Info/Warn/Error. Future contributors might bolt on Trace/Fatal without thinking about emission-site coupling.

**Solution**: Add a godoc comment to `bootstrap.Logger` noting that the four methods correspond to Run's emission levels and a fifth must not be added without a corresponding emission site.

**Outcome**: Interface contract is self-documenting.

**Do**:
1. Open `cmd/bootstrap/bootstrap.go:171-195`.
2. Add a godoc comment above the `Logger` interface explaining the four-method contract and warning against adding a fifth without a corresponding emission site.
3. Verify via `go doc cmd/bootstrap.Logger`.

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- `bootstrap.Logger` has a godoc comment explaining the four-method contract.
- Comment explicitly warns against adding a fifth method without a corresponding emission site.
- `go vet ./...` passes.

**Tests**:
- No new tests required (documentation-only). Confirm `go doc` displays the comment.

title: Phase 8: Analysis (Cycle 2)
description: Address findings from Analysis (Cycle 2).

title: Replace Local Fingerprint Helpers In composition_e2e_self_eject_integration_test.go With portaltest Helpers
description:
Problem: T6-6 (composition_e2e_self_eject_integration_test.go:442-577) authored ~135 LOC of fingerprint diff/format/sort helpers (assertScrollbackSnapshotsEqualSelfEjectComposite, fingerprintFieldDeltasSelfEjectComposite, formatFingerprintSelfEjectComposite, sortedSnapshotKeysSelfEjectComposite, joinDiagLinesSelfEjectComposite) concurrently with T7-1's consolidation of the same suite into internal/portaltest. Format strings are byte-identical to portaltest.FormatDelta; field branches replicate the exported DiffFingerprints switch.

Solution: Replace the assertion body with the portaltest.DiffFingerprints + portaltest.FormatDelta loop used at cmd/bootstrap/composition_abc_integration_test.go:272-284. Delete the four sibling local helpers.

Outcome: ~-135 LOC; single source of truth for fingerprint diffs across the entire cmd/bootstrap package.

Do:
1. Read composition_e2e_self_eject_integration_test.go to find the call site of assertScrollbackSnapshotsEqualSelfEjectComposite.
2. Replace its body with deltas := portaltest.DiffFingerprints(snapBefore, snapAfter); if len(deltas) > 0 { for _, d := range deltas { t.Errorf("scrollback delta: %s", portaltest.FormatDelta(d)) }; t.FailNow() } (or mirror composition_abc's exact pattern).
3. Delete the five local helpers.
4. go test -tags integration -run TestCompositeBootstrap_ExternalSaverKillTriggersSelfEject ./cmd/bootstrap/... — must pass.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Five local helper definitions removed.
- Call site uses portaltest.DiffFingerprints + portaltest.FormatDelta.
- The integration test still passes.
- Net LOC reduction >= 100.

Tests: Existing self-eject integration test must continue to pass.

title: Collapse SaverPanePID And FirstPanePIDInSession Into One Helper
description:
Problem: tmux.SaverPanePID(c, name) (saver_pane_pid.go:44, added T5-2) and tmux.Client.FirstPanePIDInSession(name) (tmux.go:575, added T4-4) both run list-panes -t =<name> -F '#{pane_pid}' and parse the first PID. Divergences: (i) -s flag presence, (ii) empty-output shape ((0, nil) vs ErrEmptyPaneList), (iii) 'no such session' sentinel wrapping. Both call sites treat any error as 'absent'; neither needs the divergent error contracts. Two failure-mode taxonomies for the same tmux call at adjacent component boundaries is an integration hazard.

Solution: Collapse to one helper. Promote SaverPanePID semantics (typed sentinels, no -s flag) to be the lone primitive; have bootstrapadapter/orphan_sweep.go call it directly. The -s distinction in FirstPanePIDInSession is NOT load-bearing for the orphan-sweep use case — verify and remove. If callers truly need -s semantics later, add a parameter.

Outcome: One helper, one error taxonomy. Adapter at orphan_sweep.go:124 no longer needs to call HasSession first (the sentinel handles absent).

Do:
1. Verify the -s flag is or isn't load-bearing for the bootstrapadapter use case — read the original task and the spec.
2. Delete Client.FirstPanePIDInSession from internal/tmux/tmux.go.
3. Update internal/bootstrapadapter/orphan_sweep.go to call tmux.SaverPanePID via errors.Is for ErrNoSuchSession / ErrEmptyPaneList → 'legitimate set empty'.
4. Remove the now-redundant HasSession pre-check.
5. Run tests + integration suite.

Acceptance Criteria:
- FirstPanePIDInSession removed from internal/tmux.
- bootstrapadapter/orphan_sweep.go uses tmux.SaverPanePID with errors.Is classification.
- All existing tests pass.

Tests: Existing orphan-sweep and saver-membership tests must continue to pass.

title: Unify The pgrep-portal-daemon Regex Pattern And Enumeration Helper
description:
Problem: The canonical regex '^portal state daemon( |$)' is declared three times (production adapter, test, internal/state/daemon_identity.go:38). The pgrep enumeration logic is duplicated between internal/bootstrapadapter/orphan_sweep.go:60-110 and cmd/bootstrap/orphan_sweep_integration_test.go:92-96, 515-549.

Solution: Promote a single exported pattern constant — most naturally state.PortalDaemonArgvPattern — so state.IdentifyDaemon's regexp.MustCompile and the adapter's pgrepDaemonPattern const both reference it. For the pgrep enumeration: since test cannot import production internals freely, promote the enumeration helper into internal/portaltest.PgrepPortalDaemons().

Outcome: One regex declaration, one pgrep enumeration.

Do:
1. Add state.PortalDaemonArgvPattern = '^portal state daemon( |$)' exported const.
2. Update internal/state/daemon_identity.go and internal/bootstrapadapter/orphan_sweep.go to reference the shared const.
3. Add portaltest.PgrepPortalDaemons() ([]int, error) mirroring the adapter's exit-1+empty-stdout semantics.
4. Update cmd/bootstrap/orphan_sweep_integration_test.go to call the portaltest helper.
5. Delete the test's local pgrepCandidatePattern const, pgrepPortalDaemonCount, and pgrepPortalDaemonPIDs.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Regex declared exactly once.
- pgrep enumeration logic declared exactly once.
- All tests pass.

Tests: Existing orphan-sweep tests must continue to pass.

title: Unify recordingLogger And captureLogger Into A Single Logger Fake
description:
Problem: recordingLogger (bootstrap_test.go:98-127) and captureLogger (orphan_sweep_integration_test.go:359-404) are both bootstrap.Logger fakes living in the same package bootstrap_test. The captureLogger docstring claims the duplication is for cross-package reasons but both are in bootstrap_test.

Solution: Delete captureLogger; extend recordingLogger with allEntries() drawing from all four level slices. Migrate captureLogger call sites.

Outcome: One fake. Net ~-45 LOC.

Do:
1. Add allEntries() []string (or whatever shape captureLogger callers consume) to recordingLogger.
2. Delete captureLogger.
3. Migrate the call sites in composition_e2e_convergence_integration_test.go:85, orphan_sweep_integration_test.go:240, etc.

Acceptance Criteria: One Logger fake remains in package bootstrap_test. All existing tests pass.

Tests: Existing tests using either fake must continue to pass.

title: Replace Sorted-Map-Keys Helpers With slices.Sorted + maps.Keys
description:
Problem: Three trivial 'collect keys, sort.Strings, return' helpers (sortedSnapKeys, sortedKeys, sortedSnapshotKeys) exist across three integration test files. slices.Sorted(maps.Keys(m)) (Go 1.21+) removes the need for any of them.

Solution: Replace each call site inline; delete the helpers.

Outcome: -3 helpers; idiomatic stdlib usage.

Do:
1. Replace each helper call with slices.Sorted(maps.Keys(m)) inline.
2. Delete the three helper declarations.

Acceptance Criteria: All three helpers removed. All tests pass.

Tests: Existing tests must continue to pass.

title: Consolidate portal_saver.go Seams Into Seam Structs With One Setter Idiom
description:
Problem: internal/tmux/portal_saver.go has ~18 package-level mutable seams + ~13 *Seam() accessors + two Set* setter functions. Three different setter idioms within one file: (a) bare var with no setter, (b) Set* function with nil-guard, (c) *Seam() returning *Func for direct write. Cumulative surface is the largest implicit-dependency cluster in the codebase.

Solution: Bundle related seams into one or two seam structs (e.g. killBarrierSeams{...}, saverReadinessSeams{...}) so tests swap whole clusters atomically. Pick ONE setter idiom uniformly.

Outcome: Cosmetic; materially lowers the file's cognitive load. Tests update to swap structs instead of individual vars.

Do:
1. Identify related seam clusters (kill-barrier, readiness-barrier, version, etc.).
2. Bundle each cluster into a struct.
3. Replace setter idioms uniformly (recommend the *Seam() returning pointer pattern; check existing precedent before deciding).
4. Update tests accordingly.

Acceptance Criteria: Seam clusters bundled. One setter idiom across the file.

Tests: All existing tests must continue to pass.

title: Rename killBarrierLogger To saverBarrierLogger
description:
Problem: waitForSaverDaemonReady (Component F readiness barrier — distinct from the kill barrier) emits its timeout WARN through killBarrierLogger.Warn(...) (portal_saver.go:227, 440). Name lies about scope.

Solution: Rename to saverBarrierLogger (or portalSaverLogger) with SetBarrierLogger / BarrierLogger renamed in lockstep.

Outcome: Maintainer searching for readiness-barrier WARN emission can find it via the broader sink name.

Do:
1. Rename killBarrierLogger → saverBarrierLogger in portal_saver.go.
2. Rename setter and interface as needed.
3. Update production wiring in bootstrapadapter and any other call sites.
4. Update tests.

Acceptance Criteria: No reference to killBarrierLogger remains. All tests pass.

Tests: All existing tests must continue to pass.

title: Replace fmt.Sprintf With strconv.Itoa In identifyPS
description:
Problem: internal/state/daemon_identity.go:56 uses fmt.Sprintf('%d', pid) for a single int, pulling fmt's reflection machinery where strconv.Itoa(pid) would format in one allocation. File's docstring emphasises low-overhead identity checks.

Solution: One-line swap.

Outcome: Marginal performance + idiomatic Go.

Do:
1. Edit daemon_identity.go:56 to use strconv.Itoa(pid).
2. Update import if needed.

Acceptance Criteria: fmt no longer imported in the file (if it was only for this call); strconv imported. All tests pass.

Tests: Existing daemon_identity tests must continue to pass.

title: Document Or Split NewIsolatedStateEnv To Reflect Parent-Env Mutation
description:
Problem: NewIsolatedStateEnv(t) (internal/portaltest/isolated_env.go:58-59) calls t.Setenv('HOME', ...) and t.Setenv('XDG_CONFIG_HOME', '') on the caller's process before returning the subprocess env-slice. Correct for the host-noise mitigation use case (fully documented) but the API conflates 'build subprocess env' with 'scrub parent env'. Constructor-shaped name understates the side-effect surface.

Solution: Pick one — (a) rename to SetupIsolatedStateEnv(t) to signal mutation, OR (b) split into scrubHostEnv(t) + BuildIsolatedEnv(t) so callers compose explicitly. (a) is lower-effort and preserves current caller call sites.

Outcome: API name better reflects side effects.

Do:
1. Pick option (a) or (b) — recommend (a) for minimal disruption.
2. Apply rename and update all callers.

Acceptance Criteria: New name in place; all callers migrated; tests pass.

Tests: Existing tests using NewIsolatedStateEnv must continue to pass under the new name.

title: Phase 9: Analysis (Cycle 3)
description: Address findings from Analysis (Cycle 3).

title: Promote Pgrep Enumeration To State.PgrepPortalDaemons
description:
**Problem**: T8-3 added `portaltest.PgrepPortalDaemons` and unified the regex const via `state.PortalDaemonArgvPattern`, but did NOT collapse the production adapter's `pgrepPortalDaemons` to use the new helper. Both functions are byte-equivalent (`exec.Command("pgrep", "-fx", state.PortalDaemonArgvPattern)`, identical exit-1-empty-stdout handling, identical parse loop). Risk: parsing changes drift silently between production and test enumerators.

**Solution**: Extract `state.PgrepPortalDaemons() ([]int, error)` as the single primitive. Both the bootstrapadapter and portaltest helpers become thin forwarders.

**Outcome**: One pgrep enumeration implementation. Production and test cannot drift.

**Do**:
1. Add `state.PgrepPortalDaemons() ([]int, error)` in `internal/state/daemon_identity.go` (or sibling file in state). Implementation lifts the existing logic from `internal/bootstrapadapter/orphan_sweep.go:75-109`.
2. Refactor `internal/bootstrapadapter/orphan_sweep.go`'s `pgrepPortalDaemons` to forward to `state.PgrepPortalDaemons`.
3. Refactor `internal/portaltest/pgrep.go`'s `PgrepPortalDaemons` to forward to `state.PgrepPortalDaemons`.
4. Run tests.

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- `state.PgrepPortalDaemons` exists with the canonical pgrep logic.
- Both `internal/bootstrapadapter/orphan_sweep.go` and `internal/portaltest/pgrep.go` are forwarders.
- All tests pass.

**Tests**: Existing orphan-sweep and pgrep-using tests must continue to pass.

Sources: duplication-c3#1, architecture-c3#4

title: Add Tmux.SaverPanePIDOrAbsent Helper Centralising Any Error → Absent Rule
description:
**Problem**: The "what PID owns the `_portal-saver` pane?" question is asked from 3 sites, each decoding `tmux.SaverPanePID`'s rich sentinel contract differently. The "treat any error as absent" rule lives in prose comments rather than a typed helper.

**Solution**: Add `SaverPanePIDOrAbsent(c, name) (pid int, present bool, err error)` in `internal/tmux/` that owns the sentinel collapse. Refactor both Component B's adapter and Component D's probe to consume it.

**Outcome**: One helper owns the absent-collapse policy. Callers express only their own response to `present == false`.

**Do**:
1. Add `tmux.SaverPanePIDOrAbsent(c, name) (pid int, present bool, err error)` in `internal/tmux/saver_pane_pid.go`. Implementation calls `SaverPanePID`, treats `ErrNoSuchSession`/`ErrEmptyPaneList` as `(0, false, nil)`, other errors as `(0, false, err)`, success as `(pid, true, nil)`.
2. Refactor `internal/bootstrapadapter/orphan_sweep.go::saverPanePID` to use the new helper.
3. Refactor `cmd/state_daemon.go::defaultSaverMembershipProbe` to use the new helper.
4. Run tests.

**Acceptance**: Both call sites delegate the absent-collapse to the new helper. All tests pass.

**Tests**: Existing orphan-sweep and self-supervision tests must continue to pass.

Sources: architecture-c3#1

title: Delete waitForAnyDaemonPID (Functionally Identical To waitForDaemonPID)
description:
**Problem**: `waitForDaemonPID` (upgrade_path_integration_test.go) and `waitForAnyDaemonPID` (composition_e2e_harness_integration_test.go) are functionally identical. `waitForAnyDaemonPID`'s docstring claims it is "distinct" but the body enforces `pid == orphanPID` exactly like `waitForDaemonPID`.

**Solution**: Delete `waitForAnyDaemonPID`; have callers use `waitForDaemonPID(t, stateDir, legitimateDaemonPID)` directly.

**Outcome**: One helper. Net ~-20 LOC.

**Do**:
1. Identify callers of `waitForAnyDaemonPID` (likely 1-2 sites).
2. Replace with `waitForDaemonPID(t, stateDir, expectedPID)`.
3. Delete `waitForAnyDaemonPID` and its (incorrect) docstring.
4. Reconcile the constant pairs (`compositeOrphanPIDTimeout`/`Tick` vs `upgradePathPIDFileTimeout`/`Tick`).

**Acceptance**: One helper remains. All tests pass.

**Tests**: Existing tests must continue to pass.

Sources: duplication-c3#2

title: Promote ReadPortalLogSafe To internal/portaltest
description:
**Problem**: `readPortalLogSafe` (cmd/state_daemon_self_supervision_integration_test.go) and `readPortalLogSafeBootstrap` (cmd/bootstrap/composition_e2e_self_eject_integration_test.go) are byte-identical wrappers around `os.ReadFile(state.PortalLog(stateDir))`. The duplication is documented but never actioned.

**Solution**: Promote `ReadPortalLogSafe(stateDir string) string` to `internal/portaltest`. Both call sites drop their local defs.

**Outcome**: One helper. Net ~-20 LOC.

**Do**:
1. Add `portaltest.ReadPortalLogSafe(stateDir string) string` (consider whether it should take *testing.T to satisfy the leaf-package convention — likely yes for consistency).
2. Delete both local helpers.
3. Update ~14 call sites to use the portaltest helper.

**Acceptance**: One ReadPortalLogSafe in `internal/portaltest`. All tests pass.

**Tests**: Existing tests must continue to pass.

Sources: duplication-c3#3

title: Rename NewIsolatedStateEnv To Communicate Parent-Env Mutation In Its Name
description:
**Problem**: T8-9 documented the parent-env mutation via a "SIDE EFFECT" leading paragraph but kept the `New*` name. Architectural concern: the API SHAPE still misleads at the call site. A contributor reading `env, stateDir := portaltest.NewIsolatedStateEnv(t)` has no syntactic cue that HOME just changed.

**Solution**: Pick one — (a) rename to `IsolateStateForTest(t)` or `MustIsolateStateDir(t)` to signal mutation, OR (b) split into `ScrubHostEnv(t)` + `BuildIsolatedEnv(t)` so callers compose explicitly.

**Outcome**: API name better reflects side effects.

**Do**:
1. Pick option (a) or (b). Recommend (a) — single rename.
2. Apply rename across all ~63 call sites (use grep + bulk replace).
3. Verify tests still pass.

**Acceptance**: New name in place; all callers migrated.

**Tests**: Existing tests must continue to pass.

Sources: architecture-c3#3

title: Embed Or Collapse The 5 Saver*Seams Structs
description:
**Problem**: T8-6 created 5 separate Saver*Seams structs. `SaverSharedSeams` exists precisely because Barrier and Readiness both need `ReadPID + IdentifyDaemon` — yet the structs remain peers rather than embedded. Tests reach across two structs to drive one logical state machine.

**Solution**: Either (a) embed `SaverSharedSeams` into `SaverBarrierSeams` and `SaverReadinessSeams` so each consumer references one struct, OR (b) collapse all 5 into a single `SaverSeams` with grouped sub-fields.

**Outcome**: Cleaner test ergonomics; consumers reference one struct per code path.

**Do**:
1. Choose (a) or (b).
2. Refactor portal_saver.go.
3. Update export_test.go accessors.
4. Run tests.

**Acceptance**: One struct per consumer path (Barrier, Readiness, etc.) OR one composite struct. Tests pass.

**Tests**: Existing tests must continue to pass.

Sources: architecture-c3#2

title: Fix Component C Log-Acquire Error Level (Error → Warn Per Spec)
description:
**Problem**: Spec § Component C step 4 mandates "log WARN under ComponentDaemon and exit with status 1". Implementation at `cmd/state_daemon.go:209-211` emits `Logger.Error` instead of `Logger.Warn`. Practical impact minor (both surface in portal.log) but the deviation contradicts a verbatim spec mandate.

**Solution**: Change `Logger.Error` to `Logger.Warn`. Retain the wrapped-error return so the daemon still exits status 1.

**Outcome**: Spec verbatim compliance for the lock-acquire WARN path.

**Do**:
1. Edit `cmd/state_daemon.go:210` to use `deps.Logger.Warn` instead of `deps.Logger.Error`.
2. Run tests.

**Acceptance**: Production emits WARN for wrapped lock-acquire errors. Exit status 1 preserved.

**Tests**: If any test asserts on the log level for this path, update accordingly.

Sources: standards-c3#1

title: Phase 9: Analysis (Cycle 3)
Promote pgrep enumeration to state.PgrepPortalDaemons
description:
Problem: T8-3 added portaltest.PgrepPortalDaemons and unified the regex const via state.PortalDaemonArgvPattern, but did NOT collapse the production adapter's pgrepPortalDaemons to use the new helper. Both functions are byte-equivalent (exec.Command("pgrep", "-fx", state.PortalDaemonArgvPattern), identical exit-1-empty-stdout handling, identical parse loop). Risk: parsing changes drift silently between production and test enumerators.

Solution: Extract state.PgrepPortalDaemons() ([]int, error) as the single primitive. Both the bootstrapadapter and portaltest helpers become thin forwarders.

Outcome: One pgrep enumeration implementation. Production and test cannot drift.

Do:
1. Add state.PgrepPortalDaemons() ([]int, error) in internal/state/daemon_identity.go (or sibling file in state). Implementation lifts the existing logic from internal/bootstrapadapter/orphan_sweep.go:75-109.
2. Refactor internal/bootstrapadapter/orphan_sweep.go's pgrepPortalDaemons to forward to state.PgrepPortalDaemons.
3. Refactor internal/portaltest/pgrep.go's PgrepPortalDaemons to forward to state.PgrepPortalDaemons.
4. Run tests.

Acceptance Criteria:
- state.PgrepPortalDaemons exists with the canonical pgrep logic.
- Both internal/bootstrapadapter/orphan_sweep.go and internal/portaltest/pgrep.go are forwarders.
- All tests pass.

Tests: Existing orphan-sweep and pgrep-using tests must continue to pass.

title: Add tmux.SaverPanePIDOrAbsent Helper Centralizing Any-Error-To-Absent Rule
description:
Problem: The "what PID owns the _portal-saver pane?" question is asked from 3 sites, each decoding tmux.SaverPanePID's rich sentinel contract differently. The "treat any error as absent" rule lives in prose comments rather than a typed helper.

Solution: Add SaverPanePIDOrAbsent(c, name) (pid int, present bool, err error) in internal/tmux/ that owns the sentinel collapse. Refactor both Component B's adapter and Component D's probe to consume it.

Outcome: One helper owns the absent-collapse policy. Callers express only their own response to present == false.

Do:
1. Add tmux.SaverPanePIDOrAbsent(c, name) (pid int, present bool, err error) in internal/tmux/saver_pane_pid.go. Implementation calls SaverPanePID, treats ErrNoSuchSession/ErrEmptyPaneList as (0, false, nil), other errors as (0, false, err), success as (pid, true, nil).
2. Refactor internal/bootstrapadapter/orphan_sweep.go::saverPanePID to use the new helper.
3. Refactor cmd/state_daemon.go::defaultSaverMembershipProbe to use the new helper.
4. Run tests.

Acceptance: Both call sites delegate the absent-collapse to the new helper. All tests pass.

Tests: Existing orphan-sweep and self-supervision tests must continue to pass.

title: Delete waitForAnyDaemonPID (Functionally Identical to waitForDaemonPID)
description:
Problem: waitForDaemonPID (upgrade_path_integration_test.go) and waitForAnyDaemonPID (composition_e2e_harness_integration_test.go) are functionally identical. waitForAnyDaemonPID's docstring claims it is "distinct" but the body enforces pid == orphanPID exactly like waitForDaemonPID.

Solution: Delete waitForAnyDaemonPID; have callers use waitForDaemonPID(t, stateDir, legitimateDaemonPID) directly.

Outcome: One helper. Net ~-20 LOC.

Do:
1. Identify callers of waitForAnyDaemonPID (likely 1-2 sites).
2. Replace with waitForDaemonPID(t, stateDir, expectedPID).
3. Delete waitForAnyDaemonPID and its (incorrect) docstring.
4. Reconcile the constant pairs (compositeOrphanPIDTimeout/Tick vs upgradePathPIDFileTimeout/Tick).

Acceptance: One helper remains. All tests pass.

Tests: Existing tests must continue to pass.

title: Promote ReadPortalLogSafe to internal/portaltest
description:
Problem: readPortalLogSafe (cmd/state_daemon_self_supervision_integration_test.go) and readPortalLogSafeBootstrap (cmd/bootstrap/composition_e2e_self_eject_integration_test.go) are byte-identical wrappers around os.ReadFile(state.PortalLog(stateDir)). The duplication is documented but never actioned.

Solution: Promote ReadPortalLogSafe(stateDir string) string to internal/portaltest. Both call sites drop their local defs.

Outcome: One helper. Net ~-20 LOC.

Do:
1. Add portaltest.ReadPortalLogSafe(stateDir string) string (consider whether it should take *testing.T to satisfy the leaf-package convention — likely yes for consistency).
2. Delete both local helpers.
3. Update ~14 call sites to use the portaltest helper.

Acceptance: One ReadPortalLogSafe in internal/portaltest. All tests pass.

Tests: Existing tests must continue to pass.

title: Rename NewIsolatedStateEnv to Communicate Parent-Env Mutation in Its Name
description:
Problem: T8-9 documented the parent-env mutation via a "SIDE EFFECT" leading paragraph but kept the New* name. Architectural concern: the API SHAPE still misleads at the call site. A contributor reading env, stateDir := portaltest.NewIsolatedStateEnv(t) has no syntactic cue that HOME just changed.

Solution: Pick one — (a) rename to IsolateStateForTest(t) or MustIsolateStateDir(t) to signal mutation, OR (b) split into ScrubHostEnv(t) + BuildIsolatedEnv(t) so callers compose explicitly.

Outcome: API name better reflects side effects.

Do:
1. Pick option (a) or (b). Recommend (a) — single rename.
2. Apply rename across all ~63 call sites (use grep + bulk replace).
3. Verify tests still pass.

Acceptance: New name in place; all callers migrated.

Tests: Existing tests must continue to pass.

title: Embed or Collapse the 5 Saver*Seams Structs
description:
Problem: T8-6 created 5 separate Saver*Seams structs. SaverSharedSeams exists precisely because Barrier and Readiness both need ReadPID + IdentifyDaemon — yet the structs remain peers rather than embedded. Tests reach across two structs to drive one logical state machine.

Solution: Either (a) embed SaverSharedSeams into SaverBarrierSeams and SaverReadinessSeams so each consumer references one struct, OR (b) collapse all 5 into a single SaverSeams with grouped sub-fields.

Outcome: Cleaner test ergonomics; consumers reference one struct per code path.

Do:
1. Choose (a) or (b).
2. Refactor portal_saver.go.
3. Update export_test.go accessors.
4. Run tests.

Acceptance: One struct per consumer path (Barrier, Readiness, etc.) OR one composite struct. Tests pass.

Tests: Existing tests must continue to pass.

Solution: Change Logger.Error to Logger.Warn. Retain the wrapped-error return so the daemon still exits status 1.

Outcome: Spec verbatim compliance for the lock-acquire WARN path.

title: Phase 10: Analysis (Cycle 4)
description: Address findings from Analysis (Cycle 4).

title: Promote orphan-daemon spawn + reap-cleanup helpers to internal/portaltest
description:
**Problem**: The "spawn an isolated `portal state daemon` subprocess and arrange a guaranteed reap" pattern is correctly factored inside `bootstrap_test` (via `spawnOrphanDaemonIsolated` at `cmd/bootstrap/composition_e2e_harness_integration_test.go:382-394` and `registerSubprocessCleanup` at `cmd/bootstrap/orphan_sweep_integration_test.go:365-381`), but is reimplemented verbatim in two `internal/tmux/*_integration_test.go` files because the helpers cannot be cross-imported from `bootstrap_test`. The reap-goroutine block in `internal/tmux/kill_barrier_escalation_no_final_flush_integration_test.go:191-207` is line-for-line equivalent to `registerSubprocessCleanup`, and `internal/tmux/portal_saver_endstate_integration_test.go:312-334` inlines a simpler Kill+Wait variant of the same envelope. The load-bearing rationale comments (darwin `comm`-match requires unqualified `"portal"` argv[0]; SIGKILL belt-and-braces; errors swallowed because the daemon is typically already exited) are triplicated.
